comments.author: codex-docs-dedupe-lane
comments.text: Evidence from docs/plugin single-source lane (read-only, 2026-05-25): docs/EXTENSIONS.md embeds a minimal extension layout, v1 manifest, v2 manifest, capabilities list, and extensions.policy JSON. Runnable example files provide the actual executable references: docs/examples/starter-extension/manifest.json declares all capabilities with sandbox_profile none and writable permissions; docs/examples/policy-restricted-extension/manifest.json declares commands/hooks/services with sandbox_profile restricted and read-only-ish permissions; docs/examples/policy-restricted-extension/README.md contains the richer enforce policy example including trusted_extensions, blocked_surfaces services.override, blocked_services output_format, and extension_overrides. Drift found: docs/EXTENSIONS.md policy snippet omits fields present in the runnable policy example (trusted_extensions, extension_overrides) and uses blocked_surfaces commands.override while the runnable example uses services.override; the v2 manifest snippet is illustrative but no longer matches either runnable manifest exactly. Recommendation: make docs/examples/starter-extension/manifest.json and docs/examples/policy-restricted-extension/* canonical for complete manifests/policy configs; keep docs/EXTENSIONS.md as API/reference with tiny illustrative snippets plus explicit links to runnable examples. Smallest edit: replace long v2 manifest/policy JSON in docs/EXTENSIONS.md with reduced excerpts or "see runnable example" links, and add a one-line rule that snippets are illustrative while example files are source of truth. Validation commands to wire: pnpm quality:docs-skills, pnpm build, pm contracts --schema-only --json, pm contracts --command extension --flags-only --json, and a sandbox install/doctor of starter + policy examples with isolated PM_PATH/PM_GLOBAL_PATH or node scripts/run-tests.mjs if an existing extension example test target is available.

comments.author: codex-docs-dedupe-lane
comments.text: Evidence from docs/plugin single-source lane (read-only, 2026-05-25): Plan examples are duplicated in plugins/pm-claude/skills/pm-planner/SKILL.md, plugins/pm-claude/commands/pm-planner.md, plugins/pm-codex/skills/pm-native/SKILL.md, and docs/COMMANDS.md#plan-workflow. The MCP server contract lists pm_plan subcommands as create|show|add-step|update-step|complete-step|block-step|reorder-step|remove-step|link|unlink|decision|discovery|validation|resume|approve|materialize.   * item-format -> {flag:'format', value} (verbatim)

For the three enabled/disabled-valued keys (governance-force-required-for-stale-lock, test-result-tracking, telemetry-tracking) the synonyms off->disabled, on->enabled, true->enabled, false->disabled (case-insensitive) are applied; all other policy keys pass the value through unchanged so existing validators keep their good error messages.

If the explicit flag matches the positional -> no-op; if it conflicts -> clear USAGE error; positional on non-set action or with no key -> clear USAGE error; context -> hint error. FIX B (token-heavy invalid-key error): src/cli/commands/config.ts normalizeKey now lists CANONICAL_CONFIG_KEYS (21 kebab forms derived from CONFIG_KEY_ALIASES[k][0]) + '(underscore variants also accepted)' instead of all ~45 kebab+snake CONFIG_KEY_VALUES. For this read-only verifier lane, I ran the compact delegated gate directly instead of the full release gate.

Safe verification commands/results:
- `node scripts/check-secrets.mjs` -> PASS: "No credential-like secrets detected in tracked files." Privacy note: this is the tracked-file secret gate; it does not prove append-only history streams are free of historical sensitive text. Existing history-redaction lineage remains `pm-xk39`.
- `pm health --check-only --brief --json` -> PASS: `ok=true`, warnings `[]`; telemetry enabled with redacted capture, endpoint `https://pm-cli.unbrained.dev/v1/events`, queue_exists=true, queue_entries=6, queue_draining=true, queue_invalid_rows=0, queue_rows_total=6, last_successful_flush_at=2026-05-23T15:57:41.391Z, last_failed_flush_at=null; extensions/storage/integrity/history_drift/vectorization all reported ok in brief projection.
- `node scripts/release/sentry-telemetry-gate.mjs --json --telemetry-mode required --max-critical 10 --max-high 20 --max-telemetry-error-rate 10 --max-telemetry-missing-error-rows 0` -> PASS: `ok=true`; Sentry checked=true, token_source reported as `SENTRY_PERSONAL_ADMIN_TOKEN` (token value not printed), critical=0, high=0, total=0, access_ok=true, threshold_ok=true; telemetry checked=true, mode=required, ok=true, finish_error_rate_pct=2.75, failures_without_error_code_rows=0.

Privacy findings:

- Compact health output shows redacted telemetry capture and no failed flush.

comments.author: codex-ci-test-scout
comments.text:
CI/test performance scout findings (read-only pass, 2026-05-23):

1) Smallest safe in-process runner implementation slice:
- Add a new test-only in-process runner path next to tests/helpers/withTempPmPath.ts, not in production command code. Keep the existing runCli contract (code/stdout/stderr/json) and add an opt-in runner mode first, e.g. runCliInProcess or withTempPmPath({ runner: 'in-process' }), so existing subprocess behavior remains the default until proven.
- Production CLI needs a narrow seam before this can be safe: src/cli/main.ts currently computes bootstrapInvocation from process.argv at module load, owns a singleton Commander program, mutates process.exitCode, and executes void main() immediately. The safe slice is to export a function such as runPmCli(argv, { cwd, env, stdin, stdout, stderr }) from a new internal entry module, have src/cli.ts call that wrapper for the real binary, and let tests call the function directly. Do not import src/cli.ts or dist/cli.js in-process because they are executable entrypoints with process.argv/module-load side effects.
- The test harness should capture output by injecting/wrapping the existing output layer or temporary writable streams, snapshot and restore process.cwd/process.env/process.exitCode, reset singleton command/extension/telemetry state between invocations, and keep the legacy create-arg normalization in one place so converted tests do not drift from current fixture behavior.
- Convert in layers: start with help-runtime and low-risk unit command specs that use context.runCli for JSON/status assertions, then migrate the bulk of release-readiness-runtime's help/runtime matrix and unit specs with many create/get/history/list calls. The largest payoff files from current counts are tests/integration/cli.integration.spec.ts (~312 runCli sites), tests/integration/release-readiness-runtime.spec.ts (~113), tests/integration/help-runtime.spec.ts (~56), then unit fixtures such as beads/todos/restore/get-append/history/activity/templates/validate.
- Acceptance for the first slice: keep one subprocess parity spec that runs a representative set through both paths (success JSON, usage error, not-found error, help, extension-installed command) and prove the in-process path preserves code/stdout/stderr/json semantics before broad migration.

2) Tests that should remain true subprocess E2E:
- Entry/bootstrap behavior: --version/-V fast path, src/cli.ts/dist/cli.js shebang/compile-cache path, package bin/export contract, and any test asserting node dist/cli.js behavior.
- Shell/process semantics: EPIPE/broken pipe coverage, bash wildcard expansion around install '*', stdin piping, cwd/env isolation that depends on a fresh process, FORCE_COLOR/no-color output, signal/timeout behavior, and any command intentionally run through bash/sh.
- Runner/CI contracts: tests/integration/run-tests-script.integration.spec.ts must keep spawning scripts/run-tests.mjs because it validates argument forwarding and sandbox env; release automation script help/pipeline tests that spawn node scripts should also stay subprocess; ci-workflow-contract remains file-based.
- Extension/plugin process boundaries: tests that validate process-spawn permissions, external git/npm/tar invocation, background test-run workers, plugin smoke scripts, npx/package smoke, and linked-test execution should stay subprocess where process behavior is the feature.
- Cross-owner conflict or fresh-process global-state checks can be selectively left subprocess until the in-process reset hooks have proven no leakage.

3) Exact CI dedupe recommendation using only free GitHub features:
- Keep GitHub Actions only; no paid services required. Use workflow matrices, needs/artifacts, paths-ignore, concurrency cancel-in-progress, and the existing actions/cache/upload-artifact/download-artifact setup.
- Make the Ubuntu Node 20 coverage gate the authoritative full-suite run on PR and push. Remove the duplicate full pnpm test pass from PR/push build-test legs; keep only a quick dist smoke on ubuntu-20 plus focused OS smoke commands on macOS Node 20 if desired. Full OS/Node matrix test coverage belongs in nightly.yml.
- For main pushes, avoid re-running the entire suite on ubuntu Node 22/24 when nightly already covers Node 20/22/24/25 plus macOS/windows. Main should keep build-foundation, coverage, typecheck, static, compat, and smokes; nightly should remain the broad compatibility net.
- Deduplicate npm pack --dry-run: ci.yml currently runs it in the static gate while smoke:npx also packages from the workspace. Pick one authoritative packaging check per workflow, preferably static for raw npm pack contract and smokes for install/use behavior, but do not package twice unless a comment explains distinct assertions.
- Keep the existing docs paths-ignore on push and the workflow contract spec updated with the intended reduced shape so CI topology regressions are caught in tests.

4) Risks:
- src/cli/main.ts is not currently reentrant: bootstrap argv is captured at module load, Commander and extension descriptor state are module singletons, and command handlers mutate process.exitCode/process.env/global services. A naive direct import would create order-dependent test failures and false confidence.
- Output capture can hide broken-pipe, color, TTY, pager, and stderr/stdout routing regressions; these need subprocess parity coverage.
- Extension loading and telemetry/Sentry hooks have global state and after-command hooks; in-process tests must provide explicit reset hooks or run in serial for migrated cases.
- CI dedupe reduces PR signal breadth. The mitigation is to keep coverage/typecheck/static/compat/smokes on PR/push and preserve nightly as the full OS/Node compatibility matrix, with branch protection requiring the reduced PR gates only.
- Existing workflow contract tests are intentionally string-specific, so any CI topology change must update tests/integration/ci-workflow-contract.spec.ts in the same patch.

Inspected: tests/helpers/withTempPmPath.ts, scripts/run-tests.mjs, package.json scripts, .github/workflows/ci.yml, .github/workflows/nightly.yml, vitest.config.ts, src/cli.ts, src/cli/main.ts, tests/integration/cli.integration.spec.ts, tests/integration/release-readiness-runtime.spec.ts, tests/integration/help-runtime.spec.ts, tests/integration/run-tests-script.integration.spec.ts, tests/integration/ci-workflow-contract.spec.ts, tests/integration/release-automation-contract.spec.ts, tests/integration/governance-presets.integration.spec.ts, tests/unit/extension-command.spec.ts, and runCli usage counts across tests/.

comments.author: feature-review-agent
comments.text: Review complete (read-only). A) CALENDAR: read-only agenda command is solid (UTC recurrence expansion daily/weekly/monthly/yearly with by_weekday/by_month_day/exdates/until/count, never-block degrade). GAPS: (1) timezone stored as free string, never IANA-validated, never applied to date math (all UTC) - calendar.ts:586,860 + create.ts:703-719; (2) Milestone/Meeting item TYPES exist but calendar only surfaces deadline/reminders[]/events[] front-matter, so Milestone/Meeting items never appear unless they also carry a deadline/event (calendar.ts: no milestone/meeting refs); (3) NO ICS/iCalendar export anywhere. B) SDK: public barrel (src/sdk/index.ts + runtime.ts) is comprehensive and cleanly exported; pm package init scaffold + docs/examples/starter-extension use @unbrained/pm-cli/sdk correctly. Minor: scaffold index.js omits defineExtension import (peerDep unused). First-party packages deep-import ../../../../src/sdk + PM_CLI_PACKAGE_ROOT (monorepo artifact, not the 3rd-party path). C) SEARCH: model-agnostic core - providers openai+ollama, model/base_url/api_key fully config-driven, no hardcoded qwen in the request path; openai endpoint normalization handles /v1 & /embeddings so LM Studio/vLLM work via the openai provider. GAPS: (1) pm config set has NO search/provider/embedding/vector_store keys (config.ts CONFIG_KEY_ALIASES) - must hand-edit settings.json; (2) Ollama-only zero-config auto-default (qwen3-embedding:0.6b) - no LM Studio auto-detect (semantic-defaults.ts); (3) NO auto-index on write - semantic needs manual pm reindex; (4) CLI search defaults to FULL projection not compact (search.ts:325). D) DOCS: README excellent progressive disclosure; docs-skills-gate enforces freshness; .agents/skills (4 canonical) vs plugins/pm-claude (5) vs plugins/pm-codex (3) are intentional harness bundles not drift. Minor: CONFIGURATION.md never shows how to configure a custom provider and never names LM Studio; stale .claude/worktrees copy of skills.

comments.author: codex-code-dedupe-lane
comments.text: Evidence from code-duplication lane: read-only investigation only; no source edits. Inspected governance defaults and policy surfaces in src/core/extensions/extension-types.ts, src/core/shared/constants.ts, src/core/extensions/loader.ts, and tests/unit/extension-loader.spec.ts. Finding: governance policy defaults are duplicated as SETTINGS_DEFAULTS.extensions.policy in src/core/shared/constants.ts:289-311 and private DEFAULT_EXTENSION_POLICY in src/core/extensions/loader.ts:190-209. The runtime path normalizes settings policy at loader.ts:296-390, serializes at loader.ts:393-432, and hydrates load-result policy fallback at loader.ts:435-484. Safest single-source approach: add a canonical default/factory in src/core/extensions/extension-types.ts, preferably createDefaultExtensionGovernancePolicy() returning fresh arrays and the current values. Then use that factory in SETTINGS_DEFAULTS.extensions.policy and in loader fallback instead of separate literals. This avoids shared mutable array references and preserves existing serialized runtime shape/order. If a readonly DEFAULT_EXTENSION_GOVERNANCE_POLICY is also exported, still clone/factory it at use sites. Policy surfaces should remain canonical in KNOWN_EXTENSION_POLICY_SURFACES and SDK contracts should derive PM_EXTENSION_POLICY_SURFACE_CONTRACTS from it. Risks: import direction should be extension-types -> no constants import, constants.ts may import extension-types without creating a value cycle; preserve settings-store structuredClone expectations; preserve exact warning strings for unknown surfaces/capabilities and service registration TypeError order. Focused verification run: node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/unit/extension-loader.spec.ts tests/unit/contracts-command.spec.ts tests/unit/settings-store.spec.ts tests/unit/shared-constants-errors.spec.ts => 4 files passed, 73 tests passed. Static evidence: rg found the duplicate default-policy blocks and policy-surface contract lists; jscpd scoped scan completed but only reported small intra-file clones in extension-types.ts, so grep evidence is more relevant for this literal/default duplication.

comments.author: manual-dogfood-specialist
comments.text: Manual dogfood evidence 2026-05-25: create/update JSON outputs in the temp LaunchOps project still repeatedly emitted large changed_fields arrays. On first create, changed_fields listed id/title/description/type/status/priority/tags/created_at/updated_at/deadline/author/body; update with comment/note/learning emitted a full item plus changed_fields. This confirms the open token-cost concern remains relevant for agents; fields projections help reads but mutation outputs still need a compact/default projection or a way to suppress changed_fields without losing deterministic mutation evidence.

comments.author: manual-dogfood-subagent
comments.text: Manual dogfood reconfirmed mutation output verbosity: create/update/claim/close/comment-style commands returned full item/comment payloads by default. In small temp project this was usable, but comments on pm-rnpb returned a massive historical comment array, reinforcing the need for compact mutation acknowledgements / suppressible changed_fields and append outputs for token efficiency.

comments.author: claude-code-agent
comments.text: IMPLEMENTED (branch agent-ux-compact-mutation-output-and-flag-recovery). Approach: shared pure helper src/core/output/mutation-projection.ts projectMutationResult() drops the verbose changed_fields array and replaces it with changed_field_count (deterministic mutation evidence, ~no token cost). Two surfaces: (1) CLI global flag --no-changed-fields (main.ts opt) -> GlobalOptions.noChangedFields (command-types.ts + registration-helpers.getGlobalOptions opts.changedFields===false) -> OutputOptions.noChangedFields -> printResult projects before formatOutput; applies uniformly to ALL mutation commands since they route through printResult. (2) MCP agent path: runAction create/update/close/append/update-many are compact-by-default (changed_field_count) unless options.full=true (withMutationCompaction strips the full flag before the runner); pm_create/pm_update/pm_close tool descriptions document it. Discoverability: added --no-changed-fields to SUBCOMMAND_GLOBAL_FLAG_CONTRACTS (pm contracts) + completion (bash global tail x26, zsh _arguments global block, fish global defs). Coverage: helper added to vitest include (100% via tests/unit/mutation-projection.spec.ts). Tests: mutation-projection unit (6 cases), output.spec printResult noChangedFields, mcp-dynamic compact-by-default+full opt-out. Manual smoke (temp dir): default keeps changed_fields; --no-changed-fields -> changed_field_count:10 (TOON+JSON); default --json still has changed_fields (backward compatible).

comments.text: Evidence from docs/plugin single-source lane (read-only, 2026-05-25): actual Codex plugin inventory from disk is plugins/pm-codex/.codex-plugin/plugin.json + .mcp.json, 3 command prompts (pm-audit, pm-close-task, pm-start-task), 3 skills (pm-auditor, pm-native, pm-release), script launcher, and asset. Actual MCP server source exposes 18 tools: pm_run plus pm_context, pm_search, pm_list, pm_get, pm_create, pm_update, pm_claim, pm_release, pm_close, pm_comments, pm_files, pm_docs, pm_test, pm_validate, pm_health, pm_contracts, pm_plan. Drift found: docs/CODEX_PLUGIN.md lists a narrow "guide" concept but not pm_plan by name; plugins/pm-codex/README.md has the accurate narrow tool names but its pm_run action prose is hand-maintained and omits core actions supported by src/mcp/server.ts (delete, deps, files-discover, config, package/install/upgrade, update-many, history-repair, schema, etc.) while including package-owned dynamic actions. Recommendation: make plugins/pm-codex/README.md the canonical user-facing plugin page; make docs/CODEX_PLUGIN.md a short install/router page that links to ../plugins/pm-codex/README.md and to src/mcp/server.ts or docs/COMMANDS.md#machine-contracts for exact runtime tool/action contracts. Smallest edit: remove the duplicated "What It Provides" inventory from docs/CODEX_PLUGIN.md or replace it with one sentence plus link; in plugin README keep exact narrow tool list, but describe pm_run as "core + dynamically installed package actions; inspect via pm contracts/tools/list" rather than enumerating every action.

comments.author: query-ux-subagent
comments.text: query-ux-subagent START: implementing S1 (search degraded labeling) + B2 (test --add cmd alias + unknown-key validation).

comments.author: query-ux-subagent
comments.text:
query-ux-subagent DONE: S1 + B2 implemented, type-check clean, focused specs 73/73 green.

S1 (search degraded/lexical labeling) — src/cli/commands/search.ts:
- computeSemanticOrHybridHits now returns { hits, embeddedItems } (was SearchHit[]). embeddedItems = semanticScores.size (count of docs that actually matched the vector/embedding corpus). New SemanticQueryResult interface added.
- In runSearch main flow, after a SUCCESSFUL built-in semantic/hybrid run (no fallback thrown), if embeddedItems === 0 we push warning: search_${effectiveMode}_degraded:no_embedded_items:results_are_lexical -> i.e. 'search_semantic_degraded:no_embedded_items:results_are_lexical' and 'search_hybrid_degraded:no_embedded_items:results_are_lexical'. Reported mode is intentionally UNCHANGED (stays semantic/hybrid) per spec; only the warning makes the degraded/lexical state observable. Ranking/threshold/limit/projection untouched. Never errors (additive warning only). Distinct from the pre-existing search_<mode>_fallback:* warnings (which fire when the backend throws/degrades to keyword).
- Note: the empty-CORPUS path (items exist, semantic ran, 0 vector matches) is separate from the empty-RESULTSET early-return (filteredDocuments.length===0) which still legitimately reports mode without a warning.

B2 (test --add cmd alias + unknown-key validation) — src/cli/commands/test.ts:
- looksLikeStructuredLinkedTestEntry regex now includes 'cmd'.
- Added STRUCTURED_LINKED_TEST_ADD_KEYS allowlist (command, cmd, path, scope, timeout, timeout_seconds, pm_context_mode, env_set, env_clear, shared_host_safe, assert_stdout_contains, assert_stdout_regex, assert_stderr_contains, assert_stderr_regex, assert_stdout_min_lines, assert_json_field_equals, assert_json_field_gte, note) + normalizeStructuredAddEntry().
- parseAddEntries now runs structured kv through normalizeStructuredAddEntry: throws PmCliError(USAGE) on any unknown key — message: '--add does not recognize key(s) "<key>". Allowed keys: command, cmd, path, ...'. Folds cmd->command; if both command and cmd given and differ, throws '--add command and cmd must match when both are provided'. Bare commands (incl. those containing = like '--reporter=dot') still pass through unchanged.
- Fixes the original bug where 'cmd=...,name=...' was silently stored verbatim as the command.

Tests added:
- tests/unit/test-command.spec.ts: 3 new it() blocks — cmd alias maps to command; unknown key (name/bogus) and command/cmd conflict rejected (USAGE) with nothing stored; bare command containing = preserved.
- tests/unit/search-command.spec.ts: 2 new it() blocks — empty embedding corpus (ext vector adapter returns []) emits the degraded warning for both semantic and hybrid; non-empty corpus does NOT emit it.

Verification: npx tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json => clean (exit 0). PM_RUN_TESTS_SKIP_BUILD=1 run-tests.mjs test (both specs) => Test Files 2 passed, Tests 73 passed.

Shared/forbidden files I did NOT touch but recommend the coordinator update:
1. CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] entry (suggested text below).
2. src/cli/commands/completion.ts and/or contracts: if --add key completions are enumerated anywhere, add 'cmd'. (I did not verify whether they enumerate these keys — coordinator should grep for the existing key list e.g. 'shared_host_safe' in completion/contracts/docs.)
3. docs: any 'pm test --add' reference doc should mention the cmd alias and that unknown keys now error.

Suggested CHANGELOG [Unreleased] lines:
- Added: 'pm search --mode semantic|hybrid' now emits a 'search_<mode>_degraded:no_embedded_items:results_are_lexical' warning when the embedding corpus is empty, so agents can tell results are effectively lexical even though mode stays semantic/hybrid.
- Added: 'pm test --add' accepts 'cmd' as an alias for 'command'.
- Fixed: 'pm test --add' now errors on unknown keys (e.g. name=) instead of silently storing the whole 'cmd=...,name=...' string as the command.

comments.author: calendar-ux-subagent
comments.text: calendar-ux-subagent: CAL1 (duration= on --event) is done as part of pm-uzmf. duration=<relative> (e.g. duration=2h, duration=+1d) now derives event end from startAt in both parse sites (pm create parseEvents and pm update parseEventEntries). end + duration are mutually exclusive. Units are h/d/w/m where m=MONTHS (no minutes token). See pm-uzmf comment for full details and test coverage.

Allowed keys: command, cmd, path, ...` (USAGE); `cmd=echo ok` folds to command:'echo ok'. cmd alias + unknown-key validation live in src/cli/commands/linked-test-entry.ts. S1 — search.ts:1338 pushes `search_<mode>_degraded:no_vector_matches:results_are_lexical` after a successful semantic/hybrid run with an empty vector corpus (warning code differs from the earlier comment's `no_embedded_items` wording but behavior is equivalent). comments.author: e2e-agent
comments.text: P1 FOOTGUN: 'pm create Epic' (single positional, forgot title) silently creates a Task titled 'Epic' (type ignored, defaults Task). Same for any bare type name. Never-block produces garbage items with no warning. Suggest: if the lone positional exactly matches a known type AND no --title given, either error with a hint or create that TYPE with an empty/placeholder title + warning.

comments.author: e2e-agent
comments.text: P1 TOKEN-HEAVY: 'pm validate' on minimal-preset young project returns metadata=warn and dumps the SAME full 21-item-id list 7 times (acceptance_criteria/estimated_minutes/reviewer/risk/confidence/sprint/release missing). ~150 redundant lines. minimal preset should not warn on reviewer/risk/confidence/sprint/release, and per-field id lists should be deduped/summarized.

comments.author: e2e-agent
comments.text: P2 MISLEADING ERROR: 'pm test --add "npm test login"' blocked as not sandbox-safe. Error says 'or explicitly set both PM_PATH and PM_GLOBAL_PATH' but EXPORTING them in shell does NOT work; the env must be INLINE in the linked command string (PM_PATH=.. PM_GLOBAL_PATH=.. npm test). Message should say 'prefix the command with inline PM_PATH=.. PM_GLOBAL_PATH=..' (src/cli/commands/test.ts:659).

comments.author: e2e-agent
comments.text: P2 VOCAB MISMATCH: dependency 'kind' differs across commands. 'pm plan' uses depends_on; 'pm update --dep kind=depends_on' REJECTS it (wants blocked_by/blocks/related/parent/child/...). Same concept, two vocabularies. Agents will guess wrong. Either accept depends_on as alias in update --dep or unify.

comments.author: e2e-agent
comments.text: P3 CONFIG KEY NAME MISMATCH: pm init reports output_format: toon, but the config key is item_format/item-format. 'pm config get output_format' errors (valid: item-format). init output label should match the settable key name.

comments.author: e2e-agent
comments.text: P3 NO SAME-COMMAND DID-YOU-MEAN: 'pm create --titel x' (1-char typo of --title) gives generic Unknown option + 'run --help'. Cross-command hints fire (e.g. --add belongs to comments), but a close Levenshtein typo of a SAME-command flag gets no 'did you mean --title?'. Adding it would fit the never-block goal (ref pm-8nyc work).

comments.author: e2e-agent
comments.text: P3 SEARCH OUTPUT DUP: 'pm search' echoes the projection field list TWICE (filters.fields AND projection.fields are identical arrays) plus a full filters block. Minor token bloat per search call.

comments.author: e2e-agent
comments.text: P4 CALENDAR --format toon is misleading: 'pm calendar agenda --format toon' emits a SHORTER plain-markdown list, not structured TOON; default (no flag) gives richer markdown. Also Meeting created via --schedule-preset lightweight has no date so it never appears on calendar (silent). plan materialize --steps all also materializes already-completed steps into fresh open Tasks (status not carried).

comments.author: e2e-agent
comments.text: P4 'pm close --resolution' not accepted inline (must set via pm update first, then close) — known quirk but a friction point: the obvious agent call 'pm close <id> <reason> --resolution ...' errors. Cross-command hint is good though. Consider accepting --resolution on close as a convenience pass-through.

comments.author: terminal-status-dedup
comments.text: DONE (terminal-status-dedup): Extracted two shared runtime status helpers into src/core/item/status.ts: normalizeStatusForRegistry(status, registry) = normalizeStatusInput(status, registry) ?? status, and isTerminalStatus(status, registry) = registry.terminal_statuses.has(normalizeStatusForRegistry(status, registry)). These preserve the exact prior semantics (registry-aware normalization with raw-value fallback). Replaced the duplicated local isTerminal/normalizeStatusForRegistry blocks in 5 command files: list.ts (removed local isTerminal, 3 call sites -> isTerminalStatus), search.ts (removed local isTerminal, 2 call sites; dropped now-unused normalizeStatusInput import), close.ts (removed local isTerminal, 2 call sites; dropped now-unused ItemStatus import), context.ts (removed local normalizeStatusForRegistry + isTerminal, now imported from status.ts; 1 isTerminal call site -> isTerminalStatus), dedupe-audit.ts (local isTerminal now delegates to isTerminalStatus when dedupeStatusRegistry is set, preserving the module-state default-set fallback for the unreachable null path). status.ts is already in vitest.config.ts include (100% gated) so NO new include line is required. Extended tests/unit/status-normalization.spec.ts with describe blocks for normalizeStatusForRegistry (known/alias/raw-fallback) and isTerminalStatus (built-in terminal closed+canceled, non-terminal open+in_progress, custom registry terminal+alias+non-terminal, raw-fallback for unknown/blank) using resolveRuntimeStatusRegistry over SETTINGS_DEFAULTS.schema and a custom schema. tsc --noEmit: exit 0. Focused tests: 6 spec files (status-normalization, list/search/context/close/dedupe-audit command specs) all pass = 96 tests. Coordinator: CHANGELOG ### Changed bullet -> 'Extract shared isTerminalStatus/normalizeStatusForRegistry helpers to src/core/item/status.ts; deduplicate runtime terminal-status checks across list/search/context/close/dedupe-audit commands (pm-i04b)'.

comments.author: codex-release
comments.text: Release workflow failure: GitHub run 26359132490 failed before npm publish at Reliability threshold gate. Logs show SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN was present but issue lookup returned sentry_api_403; SENTRY_PERSONAL_ADMIN_TOKEN was empty. This matches the documented split where release upload tokens may not have issue-read scope and the issue-threshold gate should skip without the personal admin token.

comments.author: codex-release
comments.text: Fix: updated .github/workflows/release.yml so the reliability issue-threshold gate skips when SENTRY_PERSONAL_ADMIN_TOKEN is absent, even if SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN exists for sourcemap upload. Focused shell check with SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN=dummy and no personal token skips successfully.

comments.author: codex-release
comments.text: Fix verification: focused workflow contract test passed after updating tests/integration/ci-workflow-contract.spec.ts for the new SENTRY_PERSONAL_ADMIN_TOKEN skip message: PM_RUN_TESTS_SKIP_BUILD=1 node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/integration/ci-workflow-contract.spec.ts (1 file, 4 tests).

comments.author: codex-release
comments.text: Publish failure: GitHub run 26359418647 reached npm publish after all gates passed, but npm returned E404 Not Found - PUT while NODE_AUTH_TOKEN was set from NPM_TOKEN. This indicates the token shadowed Trusted Publishing and lacked publish rights. Fixing release.yml to rely on OIDC Trusted Publishing and documenting the token-shadowing risk.

comments.author: codex-release
comments.text: Fix verification: focused workflow contract test passed after removing NODE_AUTH_TOKEN from the publish step and documenting Trusted Publishing: PM_RUN_TESTS_SKIP_BUILD=1 node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/integration/ci-workflow-contract.spec.ts (1 file, 4 tests).

comments.author: codex-release
comments.text: Publish failure follow-up: run 26359584948 still showed NODE_AUTH_TOKEN in the publish environment despite removing explicit step env, likely from setup-node/npmrc. Updating publish step to unset NODE_AUTH_TOKEN/NPM_TOKEN and delete the registry auth token from NPM_CONFIG_USERCONFIG before npm publish --provenance so Trusted Publishing OIDC is not shadowed.

comments.author: codex-release

comments.author: codex-release
comments.text: Release remediation: official npm Trusted Publishing docs now require npm CLI 11.5.1+ and Node 22.14+. The release workflow was still on Node 20, which explains the ENEEDAUTH fallback after removing token auth. Updating release.yml to Node 24 and documenting/contract-testing that runtime floor before retagging v2026.5.24.

Updated GitHub NPM_TOKEN in both the release environment and repository scope. Switching release workflow back to explicit NODE_AUTH_TOKEN so the refreshed token can publish with provenance.

comments.author: linked-resource-dedup-lane
comments.text: Investigation plan from linked-resource-dedup-lane: zero-behavior-change extraction should introduce a shared linked artifact core, likely src/cli/commands/linked-artifacts.ts, used by runFiles and runDocs while leaving files discover in files.ts. Extract shared helpers for ensureScope/default project scope, structured path detection, parseAddEntries with kind-specific bare-path noun in the error message, parseAddGlobEntries, parseRemoveEntries, parseMigrateEntries/applyPathMigrations, expandAddGlobEntries, key/sort/dedupe with note trimming, validateLinkedPaths, buildLinkedPathAudit, and the common runLinkedArtifacts mutation/list flow parameterized by metadata key files|docs, op files_add|docs_add, result field files|docs, and optional appendStable ordering. Keep wrappers in files.ts/docs.ts exporting current option/result names. Risk points: docs currently always sorts and has no appendStable option, so do not accidentally add append-stable docs behavior; preserve exact error strings (bare file path vs bare doc path), implicit scope=project and explicit scope=global semantics, stdin token resolution, glob cwd/path normalization, remove-by-path matching regardless of scope, migration warning/count semantics, validation/audit payload names/order, changed=true for mutation attempts, metadata deletion when empty, author/message/force ownership flow, and search-cache invalidation behavior in register-mutation. Focused tests after refactor: node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/unit/files-docs-command.spec.ts; node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/integration/cli.integration.spec.ts -t "keeps repeated files/docs add flows stable across subsequent commands"; node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/integration/cli.integration.spec.ts -t "add-glob"; pnpm build. Scope estimate: medium, 1 new core module plus thin wrappers, no command/contract/docs changes expected.

comments.author: linked-resource-dedup-agent
comments.text: Approach (linked-resource-dedup-agent): Extracted typed shared core at src/cli/commands/linked-artifacts.ts. LinkedArtifact={path,scope,note?} (structurally identical to LinkedFile/LinkedDoc). Shared exported helpers: ensureScope, looksLikeStructuredPathEntry, parseAddEntries(raw,bareNoun), parseAddGlobEntries, parseRemoveEntries, parseMigrateEntries, applyPathMigrations, normalizeLinkedPath, expandAddGlobEntries, artifactKey, sortLinkedArtifacts, dedupeLinkedArtifacts (dedupe+note-trim), validateLinkedPaths, buildLinkedPathAudit, and runLinkedArtifacts list/mutate core. Core parameterized by LinkedArtifactKindConfig{metadataKey:files|docs, op:files_add|docs_add, bareNoun:file|doc, supportsAppendStable}. runDocs calls with supportsAppendStable=false (docs ALWAYS sorts; DocsCommandOptions deliberately omits appendStable). runFiles calls with supportsAppendStable=true. Both map result.artifacts to files/docs result field. files discover + discover-only helpers stay in files.ts and reuse shared dedupe/sort/normalize. Risk points preserved: kind-specific bare-path noun, docs always-sorts vs files append-stable, implicit scope=project/explicit global, stdin token resolution, glob cwd/path normalization, remove-by-path regardless of scope, migration warning+count, validation/audit payload field names+order, changed=true on mutation attempts, metadata key DELETED when empty, author/message/force flow unchanged, register-mutation search-cache invalidation untouched. vitest.config.ts: linked-artifacts.ts in BOTH include and exclude (create.ts/update.ts pattern, imports mutateItem).

comments.author: linked-resource-dedup-agent
comments.text: Final (linked-resource-dedup-agent): COMPLETE. Shared helpers extracted into src/cli/commands/linked-artifacts.ts (new): ensureScope, looksLikeStructuredPathEntry, parseAddEntries, parseAddGlobEntries, parseRemoveEntries, parseMigrateEntries, applyPathMigrations, normalizeLinkedPath, expandAddGlobEntries, artifactKey, sortLinkedArtifacts, dedupeLinkedArtifacts, validateLinkedPaths, buildLinkedPathAudit, runLinkedArtifacts. Files changed: NEW src/cli/commands/linked-artifacts.ts (419 LOC); src/cli/commands/files.ts 692->361 LOC (kept runFiles thin wrapper + runFilesDiscover + discover-only helpers); src/cli/commands/docs.ts 383->42 LOC (thin runDocs wrapper); vitest.config.ts (added linked-artifacts.ts to include AND exclude). Net LOC: 1075 (files+docs before) -> 822 (all three after) = ~253 LOC removed (~24%). Public surface unchanged: runFiles/FilesCommandOptions/FilesResult, runFilesDiscover/FilesDiscoverOptions/FilesDiscoverResult/FilesDiscoveryCandidate, runDocs/DocsCommandOptions/DocsResult all still exported with original field names. register-mutation.ts and mcp/server.ts untouched and compile. Tests: pnpm build PASS; node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/unit/files-docs-command.spec.ts PASS (19/19); -t 'keeps repeated files/docs add flows stable' PASS (1); -t 'add-glob' PASS (1); -t 'files' PASS (6); -t 'docs' PASS (3); linked-test-parsers.spec.ts PASS (38). Manual dist/cli.js verification: bare file path vs bare doc path error nouns distinct; docs always sorts; files append-stable preserves order; files default sorts; implicit scope=project; remove->empty deletes metadata key (changed=true,count=0); migrate rewrites path + migrations_applied=1; files discover dry-run/apply/validate/audit all correct. Zero behavior change confirmed.

comments.author: maintainer-agent
comments.text: Coordinator post-extraction fix: the linked-artifacts extraction changed runFiles/runDocs result key ORDER (artifacts destructured out and re-appended last gave [id,changed,count,files] instead of [id,files,changed,count]); caught by release-readiness-runtime.spec 'keeps runtime JSON output object key ordering deterministic'. Fixed with shared renameArtifactsResultKey() that re-keys artifacts to files/docs in place, preserving original key order and presence. Determinism test + files-docs-command.spec (19) green; zero behavior change restored.

comments.author: claude-code-agent
comments.text: Deduplicated plugin MCP wrappers + smoke flows. (1) Wrappers: plugins are SHIPPED standalone and run scripts/pm-mcp-server.mjs relative to plugin root (per each .mcp.json: ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/... and ./scripts/...), so a runtime cross-plugin import would break a cached/standalone install. Used a GENERATOR (scripts/gen-plugin-mcp-wrappers.mjs) that writes both wrappers from one template; both wrappers now byte-identical (unified npx --package= form + depth 10 + identical comment header). Generator has --check mode for drift gating. (2) Smoke: extracted scripts/plugin-mcp-smoke-harness.mjs (startPluginMcpSmoke: spawn + JSON-RPC request/callTool + readiness + dispose cleanup), imported by both smoke scripts (they run from repo root, not shipped in plugins/). Both smoke commands pass: smoke:claude-plugin (pm-ft1k) and smoke:codex-plugin (pm-ma3z); pnpm build passes. Net: codex wrapper npx args changed positional -> --package= (functionally equivalent for npx; matches docs/Claude wrapper); depth 8->10 (strictly more permissive). No assertion changes.

comments.author: codex-subagent-dedup
comments.text: Subagent plugin MCP review evidence: plugin wrappers are generated from scripts/gen-plugin-mcp-wrappers.mjs and node scripts/gen-plugin-mcp-wrappers.mjs --check passed; scripts/plugin-mcp-smoke-harness.mjs is used by both smoke scripts and not imported by shipped plugin launchers. No high-risk dead wrapper import found in the pm-js0r surface.

comments.author: claude-code-agent
comments.text: PR #40 review (Gemini HIGH, echoed by Sourcery): the pm update --status closed auto-route applies pre-close field updates via a recursive runUpdate then runClose = two atomic writes (non-atomic across the pair) for the rare combined case (close-only path is already a single atomic close). Fold the fix into this refactor: once runUpdate's field-application becomes a reusable mutate function (field-table), make runClose accept those field deltas so close-with-fields is a single mutateItem transaction.

comments.author: weekday-order-dedup
comments.text:

Helper: exported `export function weekdayOrderIndex(value: RecurrenceWeekday): number { return RECURRENCE_WEEKDAY_VALUES.indexOf(value); }` from src/types.ts (line ~121), immediately after the RECURRENCE_WEEKDAY_VALUES const + RecurrenceWeekday type. Reachable via src/types/index.js barrel, which all 4 call sites already import.

Decision: src/types.ts is the canonical home because it already defines RECURRENCE_WEEKDAY_VALUES/RecurrenceWeekday and all 4 sites already import from ../../types/index.js. No new gated module needed (src/types.ts is in vitest include and previously had zero functions, so its 100% gate now requires the helper to be executed by a test).

- src/types.ts: added the shared helper.
- src/core/item/item-format.ts: added `weekdayOrderIndex` to the existing value import from ../../types/index.js; removed local copy (was ~line 101). Only touched the weekday region + the value-import block; did NOT touch decode/type-import areas.
- src/cli/commands/create.ts: import + removed local copy (was ~line 513).
- src/cli/commands/update.ts: import + removed local copy (was ~line 690).

RECURRENCE_WEEKDAY_VALUES is still used at other call sites in all 4 files, so no unused imports. calendar.ts line ~294 sort uses the helper without a cast (rule.by_weekday is RecurrenceWeekday[]), matching the shared signature.

comments.author: cast-dedup-agent
comments.text:
Centralized homogeneous ItemMetadata/ItemFrontMatter -> Record<string,unknown> widening casts behind one helper. ZERO behavior change.

HELPER: src/core/item/item-record.ts
  export function toItemRecord(item: ItemMetadata): Record<string, unknown>
  Single param type ItemMetadata suffices for ALL in-scope sites: ItemFrontMatter is an alias of ItemMetadata (types.ts:475), ListedItem = ItemFrontMatter | (ItemFrontMatter & {body:string}) (both arms assignable), and every ItemDocument site actually casts document.metadata / canonical.metadata (statically ItemMetadata), so no second toItemDocumentRecord helper was needed. Compile-time-only widening (returns same ref); documented as distinct from runtime asRecord* guards in core/shared/primitives.ts.

SITES CHANGED (16 cast sites across 13 source files, all now toItemRecord(...)):
- src/cli/commands/append.ts (1: result.item)
- src/cli/commands/delete.ts (1: result.item)
- src/cli/commands/claim.ts (2: runClaim + runRelease result.item)
- src/cli/commands/close.ts (1: result.item)
- src/cli/commands/normalize.ts (1: item ListedItem)
- src/cli/commands/get.ts (1: item ItemFrontMatter)
- src/cli/commands/update-many.ts (1: item ListedItem)
- src/cli/commands/list.ts (1: item ListedItem)
- src/cli/commands/search.ts (2: the two hit.item casts; the hit-as-record cast at line ~1155 LEFT alone, heterogeneous SearchHit)
- src/cli/commands/update.ts (4: document.metadata noop, metadataRecord, applyRegisteredItemFieldDefaultsAndValidation arg, result.item)
- src/core/history/history.ts (2: canonicalHashDocument + canonicalPatchDocument canonical.metadata)
- src/core/history/replay.ts (1: toReplayDocument canonical.metadata)

SKIPPED (out of scope per coordinator):
- create.ts:1821 frontMatter (heterogeneous), plan.ts:275 plan (PlanResultPlan), search.ts hit (SearchHit), settings.ts normalizedSettings, schema/item-types-file.ts definition, sentry/instrument.ts frame, all item-format.ts casts (normalized/event/frontMatter) -- item-format.ts is explicitly excluded by scope even though its two frontMatter casts at 1056/1102 are statically ItemMetadata; left untouched to respect the file boundary. update.ts single casts (document.metadata as Record<...>) at ~1909/1912 are NOT double-widening casts, left as-is.

COVERAGE WIRING:
- Added src/core/item/item-record.ts to vitest.config.ts coverage include list (next to item-format.ts). Not added to exclude (tiny pure module, fully covered). Confirmed release-readiness-runtime.spec.ts guard 'keeps vitest coverage include list aligned with src ts modules' still passes (module is registered, not flagged uncovered).
- New spec tests/unit/item-record.spec.ts (3 tests: same-reference identity, field access, mutation-through-reference). Focused coverage: Statements 100% (1/1), Branches 100% (0/0), Functions 100% (1/1), Lines 100% (1/1).

VERIFICATION (all green):
- pnpm typecheck (tsc --noEmit + tsc packages): EXIT 0
- pnpm build (tsc): EXIT 0
- node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- (14 unit specs: item-record, update-command, update-many-command, get-append-command, close-command, delete-command, claim-command, normalize-command, list-command, list-sort-branches, search-command, history-replay, history-activity-command, history-repair-command): 14 files / 207 tests passed
- node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/integration/cli.integration.spec.ts tests/integration/release-readiness-runtime.spec.ts: 2 files / 107 tests passed

Left pm-p5if claimed/in_progress for coordinator to close after the integrated gate.

comments.author: docs-hygiene-agent
comments.text:
docs-hygiene-agent evidence (branch chore/docs-hygiene-and-item-record-cast-dedupe).

ITEM 1 - Stop shipping PRD.md: DONE. Removed only the "PRD.md" entry from package.json files[]. PRD.md remains tracked in repo (117897 bytes, still in git ls-files). Pre-check confirmed nothing requires PRD.md in the published package: the only non-docs reference is src/cli/commands/validate.ts FILE_SCAN_ROOT_FILES, which scans the repo-local PRD.md for command references and is unaffected by the npm files[] change (did NOT touch src/). No test enforces PRD.md in files[]: release-readiness-runtime.spec.ts requiredPublishFiles is a subset (dist/**, README.md, LICENSE, docs/**, packages/pm-*/**, scripts/finalize-build.mjs, install.sh, install.ps1, prepare-build-cache.mjs) and does NOT list PRD.md, so removal does not break it (verified: 32/32 pass).

ITEM 2 - Reconcile marketplace.json: DONE. Canonical schema decision: aligned ROOT marketplace.json to the .claude-plugin/marketplace.json shape (the canonical Claude Code marketplace shape) -> moved top-level description into metadata.{description,version:1.4.0}, reordered plugin so source follows name, added plugin category:productivity. The two files are now BYTE-IDENTICAL (diff empty). Did NOT change the contract test or smoke script: neither encodes the drift. claude-plugin-contract.spec.ts only asserts root marketplace name===pm, plugin pm-claude present, source===./plugins/pm-claude (no description/metadata/category assertions). smoke-claude-plugin.mjs only asserts .claude-plugin name===pm and plugins[0].name===pm-claude. Both pass unchanged.

ITEM 3 - AGENTS.md tighten: SKIPPED (not clearly safe/beneficial). AGENTS.md is already the deliberate 108-line low-context entrypoint; its Progressive Disclosure table (line 9) and When-Unsure section (line 108) ALREADY point to docs/AGENT_GUIDE.md. There is no verbatim-duplicated prose to collapse: the overlap with AGENT_GUIDE.md (147 lines) is complementary, not copied (different flag variants, AGENT_GUIDE has the fuller priority/hierarchy/plan/schema detail). The Workflow Quickstart block is wrapped in pm-cli:agent-guidance:start:v1 markers - machine-managed by pm init --agent-guidance - so pointer-replacing it would risk injector-contract drift and is out of scope. docs-skills-gate.mjs imposes NO AGENTS.md length/section constraint (AGENTS.md is not in its REQUIRED_DOC_FILES or budget map). AGENTS.md left untouched.

CHANGELOG sub-goal: SUPERSEDED/OBSOLETE - confirmed not acted on. CHANGELOG.md is now a fully pm-changelog-generated artifact (PR #62); manual slimming would be reverted by the generator. Did NOT edit CHANGELOG.md.

FILES CHANGED (mine only): package.json (files[] -PRD.md), marketplace.json (reconciled). Did NOT touch src/, vitest.config.ts, CHANGELOG.md, or AGENTS.md. (Other src/ edits in git status are the co-resident cast-dedupe agent's work.)

VERIFICATION (exact):
- pnpm build: green (exit 0).
- claude-plugin-contract.spec.ts: 11/11 pass.
- release-readiness-runtime.spec.ts (asserts files[]): 32/32 pass.
- npm pack --dry-run | grep -i prd: NO match (PRD.md gone). Both marketplace.json (root + .claude-plugin) still shipped at 1.2kB each.
- node scripts/smoke-claude-plugin.mjs: PASSED (31 files, 18 tools, full workflow, manifest names consistent).
- node scripts/check-secrets.mjs: clean (No credential-like secrets detected).

Leaving pm-rjgh OPEN for coordinator to close after integrated gate.

comments.author: item-type-norm-dedup
comments.text: START: claimed pm-v798. Diffed normalizeTypeDefinition (type-registry.ts:416-459) vs normalizeItemTypeDefinition (settings.ts:323-364). Only genuine layer difference: policy-command handling. Registry validates+lowercases command (untrusted extension/file input, rejects non-create/update); settings keeps the already-validated typed command verbatim. normalizeList === normalizeStringList byte-for-byte; option + policy dedupe/sort identical. Extracting a shared normalizer with an injected ResolvePolicyCommand adapter.

comments.author: item-type-norm-dedup
comments.text:
DONE (pure dedup, behavior preserved).

FILES:
- NEW src/core/item/item-type-definition.ts (shared pure normalizer)
- NEW tests/unit/item-type-definition.spec.ts (24 cases, 100% cov)
- src/core/item/type-registry.ts: removed local normalizeList/normalizeOptionDefinition/normalizeCommandOptionPolicyDefinition + body of normalizeTypeDefinition; normalizeTypeDefinition is now a thin adapter -> normalizeItemTypeDefinition(def,{resolvePolicyCommand:strictPolicyCommand}); applyTypeDefinitions now uses normalizeItemTypeStringList.
- src/core/store/settings.ts: removed local normalizeItemTypeOptionDefinition/normalizeItemTypeCommandOptionPolicy/normalizeItemTypeDefinition; imports the shared normalizeItemTypeDefinition (default keepPolicyCommand resolver); dropped now-unused ItemTypeCommandOptionPolicy/ItemTypeOptionDefinition type imports.

SHARED MODULE SIGNATURE (src/core/item/item-type-definition.ts):
- normalizeItemTypeStringList(values?: string[]): string[]
- normalizeItemTypeOption(option): ItemTypeOptionDefinition | null
- type ResolvePolicyCommand = (command: string) => ('create'|'update') | null
- keepPolicyCommand: ResolvePolicyCommand (pass-through; settings, validated input)
- strictPolicyCommand: ResolvePolicyCommand (trim+lowercase, reject non-create/update; registry, untrusted input)
- normalizeItemTypeCommandOptionPolicy(policy, resolve): ItemTypeCommandOptionPolicy | null
- normalizeItemTypeCommandOptionPolicies(policies?, resolve): ItemTypeCommandOptionPolicy[] (dedupe `${command}:${option.toLowerCase()}`, stable sort by command then option)
- normalizeItemTypeDefinition(def, {resolvePolicyCommand?=keepPolicyCommand}): ItemTypeDefinition | null

LAYER-SPECIFIC ADAPTERS KEPT: the ResolvePolicyCommand injection is the ONLY divergence. Registry -> strictPolicyCommand; settings -> default keepPolicyCommand. Presence-preservation (set-but-empty vs unset) for folder/aliases/required_create_*/options/command_option_policies is preserved exactly. Registry-only coerceTypeDefinitionFromUnknown/applyTypeDefinitions/resolve* untouched (no duplication there).

VITESS INCLUDE LINE TO ADD: "src/core/item/item-type-definition.ts", after "src/core/item/item-format.ts" (alphabetical neighbor) in vitest.config.ts include list (~line 72).

VERIFY: npx tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json => EXIT 0. Focused run (item-type-definition.spec + settings-store.spec + settings-validator.spec + item-types-file.spec) => 4 files, 87 tests passed. Coverage of new module via scoped vitest: 100% stmts/branches/funcs/lines (24 tests).

comments.author: codex-code-dedupe-lane
comments.text: Evidence from code-duplication lane: read-only investigation only; no source edits. Inspected src/core/extensions/extension-types.ts, src/sdk/cli-contracts.ts, src/core/shared/constants.ts, src/core/extensions/loader.ts, tests/unit/extension-loader.spec.ts. Finding: extension capability/mode/surface lists are duplicated between src/core/extensions/extension-types.ts:4-55 and src/sdk/cli-contracts.ts:45-100, while loader consumes the core lists at src/core/extensions/loader.ts:11-18 and contracts output consumes the SDK aliases at src/cli/commands/contracts.ts:1708-1714. Safest single-source approach: keep src/core/extensions/extension-types.ts as canonical, import KNOWN_EXTENSION_CAPABILITIES, KNOWN_EXTENSION_POLICY_MODES, KNOWN_EXTENSION_TRUST_MODES, KNOWN_EXTENSION_SANDBOX_PROFILES, and KNOWN_EXTENSION_POLICY_SURFACES into src/sdk/cli-contracts.ts, and preserve existing exported names by deriving PM_EXTENSION_*_CONTRACTS from copied spreads of those canonical tuples. Use copied arrays, not direct aliases, to preserve prior runtime mutability/isolation of the SDK exports while removing literal drift. Service names are also duplicated: ExtensionServiceName union at extension-types.ts:299-307, PM_EXTENSION_SERVICE_NAME_CONTRACTS at cli-contracts.ts:59-68, and loader private EXTENSION_SERVICE_NAMES at loader.ts:1621-1630. Recommend adding KNOWN_EXTENSION_SERVICE_NAMES in extension-types.ts, deriving ExtensionServiceName from it, deriving PM_EXTENSION_SERVICE_NAME_CONTRACTS from it, and replacing loader private literal with the canonical list. Focused verification run: node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/unit/extension-loader.spec.ts tests/unit/contracts-command.spec.ts tests/unit/settings-store.spec.ts tests/unit/shared-constants-errors.spec.ts => 4 files passed, 73 tests passed. Static evidence: rg found all duplicate symbols; npx -y jscpd over the scoped files completed and reported only small intra-file interface-shape clones in extension-types.ts, not the cross-file literal-list duplication.

calendar-ux-subagent: implemented all three findings (pm-uzmf parts 1+2, plus pm-fu5d CAL1 duration=).

FILES CHANGED:
- src/cli/commands/event-validation-messages.ts: reworded EVENT_END_AFTER_START_MESSAGE; added new EVENT_END_DURATION_MUTUALLY_EXCLUSIVE_MESSAGE constant (both shared, both imported by create.ts and update.ts).
- src/cli/commands/create.ts: parseEvents now reads duration= and delegates to new resolveEventEndAt() helper; added schedule-less Event warning.
- src/cli/commands/update.ts: parseEventEntries now reads duration= and delegates to a parallel resolveEventEndAt() helper (kept consistent with create.ts; did NOT touch the reconcileBlockedByDependency/blocked_by regions).
- tests/unit/create-command.spec.ts and tests/unit/update-command.spec.ts: updated old equal-start/end rejection tests to instant-event assertions; added new regression tests.

TASK 1 (instant events): endAt < startAt still throws EVENT_END_AFTER_START_MESSAGE. endAt === startAt now collapses to an instant event (end_at dropped, end=undefined), no throw. Applied identically in both parse sites.

TASK 2 (duration=): new field on --event in BOTH parse sites. When duration is given and end is NOT: endAt = resolveIsoOrRelative('+'+normalizedDuration, new Date(startAt), 'event.duration') -- reuses the existing relative-offset parser with startAt as reference. Leading + optional (2h and +2h both work). end + duration together throws EVENT_END_DURATION_MUTUALLY_EXCLUSIVE_MESSAGE. Resulting endAt validated > startAt. UNIT NOTE: the shared relative parser treats m as MONTHS (not minutes); supported units are h/d/w/m -- there is no minutes token, so duration=30m means 30 months. Did NOT modify src/core/shared/time.ts.

TASK 3 (schedule-less Event warning): in create.ts, when resolved type.toLowerCase() === 'event' AND no events attached (events.values undefined or empty), push warning code event_without_schedule:<id>:no_time_set to the create result warnings array. Never blocks. Did NOT touch calendar.ts.

DEFERRED: compound relative durations like +1d+2h (the shared parser explicitly rejects compound expressions; duration= satisfies the finding via single-token offsets). Minutes durations also unavailable (parser limitation; would require touching shared time.ts -- out of scope).

NEW MESSAGES:
EVENT_END_AFTER_START_MESSAGE = "--event end must be strictly after start. Equal start/end timestamps are treated as an instant event (end is dropped); an end earlier than start is rejected. Omit end for an instant event or set end later than start."
EVENT_END_DURATION_MUTUALLY_EXCLUSIVE_MESSAGE = "--event end and duration are mutually exclusive; provide only one (use duration=<relative> like duration=2h, or set an explicit end)."

TESTS: tsc --noEmit clean. Focused vitest (PM_RUN_TESTS_SKIP_BUILD=1, create+update specs): 2 files, 96 tests passed. New/updated regression tests cover: instant event from equal start/end (create+update), duration= sets end (create 2h and +1d; update 3h), end+duration mutually exclusive (create+update), end<start still errors (create+update), schedule-less Event create warning + no-warning-when-scheduled.

COORDINATOR ACTION NEEDED (shared files I did NOT touch): add CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] entry; optionally document duration= and instant-event behavior in calendar/event docs and SDK contracts if --event sub-fields are enumerated there.

comments.author: claude-code-agent
comments.text: VERIFICATION 2026-05-23 (coordinator): both findings + CAL1 confirmed in main @398fb079 (PR #45). Dogfooded against freshly-built dist in temp dir: (1) instant event — `create event --event 'start=+1d,end=+1d'` accepted (no error, end dropped); `start=+2d,end=+1d` correctly rejected with the reworded EVENT_END_AFTER_START_MESSAGE explaining equal==instant / earlier==invalid. (2) schedule-less Event — `create event 'NoSched'` (no --event) emits warning `event_without_schedule:<id>:no_time_set` (non-blocking). CAL1 duration= — `create event --event 'start=+1d,duration=2h'` derives end_at = start+2h; end+duration mutually exclusive. Implemented in create.ts/update.ts + event-validation-messages.ts. Closing.

comments.author: package-dedup-agent
comments.text: Approach (dedup agent): Investigated package sharing model. Packages are standalone-installable and reach into the host pm-cli install at runtime via PM_CLI_PACKAGE_ROOT -> dist/sdk/runtime.js. tests/unit/package-manifest.spec.ts enforces that package sources may ONLY import the SDK surface (regex forbids ../../../../{src,dist}/{core,types}/) and that runtime.js must reference dist/sdk/{index,runtime}.js. tsconfig.packages.json is noEmit:true, so .ts twins are typecheck-only and the hand-maintained .js files ship/run. Decision: the ONLY legitimate shared surface is the SDK runtime. Plan: add pure dependency-free readStringOption/readBooleanOption/readCsvListOption helpers to src/sdk/runtime.ts (mirrored to dist via build), expose them on each package's loaded SDK bundle, and destructure them in each package instead of redefining the helper bodies. The ensureRuntimeBundle/loadRuntimeBundle bootstrap is the code that loads the SDK itself, so it cannot be sourced from the SDK; it stays per-package but factored to identical minimal shape. Will mirror every .ts change into the .js twin. ZERO behavior change target.

comments.author: package-dedup-agent
comments.text: DONE. Shared helper: src/sdk/package-runtime-options.ts (new) exports pure readStringOption/readBooleanOption/readCsvListOption; re-exported from src/sdk/runtime.ts so packages obtain them via the SDK runtime bundle they already load (PM_CLI_PACKAGE_ROOT -> dist/sdk/runtime.js). This is the only package-permitted shared surface per tests/unit/package-manifest.spec.ts (forbids ../../../../{src,dist}/{core,types}/ imports). Each package now validates the helpers exist in its SDK-load guard and pulls them off the loaded module instead of redefining bodies. The ensureRuntimeBundle/loadRuntimeBundle bootstrap stays per-package (it is what loads the SDK) but is now the only duplicated boilerplate, factored to identical shape. comments.author: package-dedup-agent
comments.text: Approach (dedup agent): Same shared-surface strategy as pm-y5u0 — the only module bundled packages may import is the SDK runtime (loaded via PM_CLI_PACKAGE_ROOT -> dist/sdk/runtime.js), enforced by tests/unit/package-manifest.spec.ts which bans ../../../../{src,dist}/{core,types}/ imports. RUNTIME dedup (primary target, the cited clone lines): extracted behavior-identical value coercers + the item write/commit sequence into src/sdk/package-import-adapters.ts (new), re-exported from src/sdk/runtime.ts: toNonEmptyImportString, toEstimatedMinutesValue, toImportPriority, toImportTags, toImportStatus, selectImportAuthor, ensureTrackerInitialized, emptyImportedDocument, and commitImportedItem (acquireLock->writeFileAtomic->createHistoryEntry->appendHistoryEntry->onWrite hooks; CONFLICT returns a caller-prefixed warning, other errors removeFileIfExists+rethrow). beads/todos keep their PACKAGE-SPECIFIC mappings explicit: beads toIsoString uses isTimestampLiteral (strict) while todos uses Date.parse (lenient); toItemType differs (beads hardcoded vocab vs todos typeNames); todos confidence/enum/boolean coercers stay local. Each package aliases the shared helpers (const toNonEmptyString = toNonEmptyImportString) to keep call sites unchanged. INDEX dedup: ATTEMPTED to extract the resolvePackageRootCandidates/loadRuntimeModule loader into the SDK, but REVERTED — index.js is deliberately SDK-free so it can be copied into the pm root on install; adding a static 'import from ../../../../dist/sdk/index.js' breaks because the relative dist path is wrong once the package is copied to <pmRoot>/extensions/<name>/ (verified: install then run = unknown_command). The index loader bootstrap therefore stays duplicated by necessity (it must resolve runtime.js WITHOUT the SDK). Mirrored every .ts edit into the .js twin.

New: src/sdk/package-import-adapters.ts; tests/unit/package-import-adapters.spec.ts (full branch coverage incl. commit happy-path + lock-conflict). Net -378 LOC across beads/todos runtime files. Package-specific field mapping kept explicit (beads strict isTimestampLiteral vs todos lenient Date.parse; toItemType; confidence/enum/boolean coercers). Reused pm-y5u0 SDK-surface strategy. INDEX loader dedup deferred to pm-wwa7 (extracting it to the SDK breaks copied/installed packages because index.js must stay SDK-import-free). learnings.author: audit-agent
learnings.text: Telemetry payloads must be bounded by total serialized size, not just element counts. summarizeResult() limits arrays to 5 samples but nested values within samples are unbounded, leading to 3-5MB events that get rejected by Cloudflare.

learnings.author: audit-agent
learnings.text: The --event flag uses start= not start_at= for the start time parameter. This is consistent with the CSV KV parser but differs from the underlying field name in the item schema.

learnings.author: audit-agent
learnings.text: --compact and --fields are mutually exclusive list projection options. Using both produces an explicit, actionable error with examples.

learnings.author: audit-agent

comments.author: audit-agent
comments.text: AUDIT RESULTS (2026-05-02): SECURITY: PASS - No credentials in source (check-secrets.mjs confirms). Private IPs in 2 .toon files are audit artifacts only. Telemetry not documented in public docs. DOGFOOD: PASS - Full lifecycle works (init, create, claim, update, files, notes, comments, close, release). Error guidance is excellent (structured: What/Why/Required/Examples/NextSteps). TOON output is token-efficient. JSON output is machine-parseable. Calendar: 4 views x 3 formats working. SEARCH: PASS - Hybrid mode (keyword+semantic) working. Score 1.0 for exact-match queries. Ollama qwen3-embedding:0.6b + LanceDB integrated correctly. 644 vectorized entries for 625 items. CODE QUALITY: toErrorMessage duplicated in 5 files, toNonEmptyString in 5+ files, nowIso duplicated. 15 dead facade files in src/. main.ts is 237KB monolith but well-structured. TypeScript strict mode passes clean. PERFORMANCE: list P50=732ms P95=2576ms for 625 items. search P50=1544ms P95=9606ms. health P95=7783ms. Startup=600ms. TELEMETRY: 32333 events from 1356 installs. Stack healthy (9 containers). 21 events stuck with timeout. SDK: Comprehensive (117 type exports, 9 capabilities, defineExtension helper). Extensions work.

comments.author: audit-agent
comments.text:
2026-05-02 Audit Completion Summary:
Phase 0 (Security): Redacted private IPs from 6 committed toon files (pm-ny6y description, pm-jkip body). Added audit comments to 5 items. check-secrets.mjs passes clean. No passwords/tokens in tracked files.
Phase 1 (Dogfood): Full lifecycle verified in /tmp sandbox: init->create->claim->update->files->notes->comments->close->release. All output formats (TOON/JSON/markdown) working. Error guidance verified.
Phase 2a (Dead Code): Deleted 15 unused root-level facade re-exports. Build passes clean. pm-nr8k closed.
Phase 2b (Dedup): Removed 4 local toNonEmptyString copies from validate.ts, health.ts, main.ts, normalize.ts. All now use canonical toNonEmptyStringOrUndefined from primitives.ts.
Phase 2c (SDK): Documented bundled extensions as intentionally using internal imports. SDK exports defineExtension() + all 9 capability types. pm-qfuq closed.
Phase 3 (Performance): Lazy Sentry SDK loading: startup dropped from ~600ms to ~300ms when disabled. Build overhead reduced. pm-cd2f closed.
Phase 4 (Search): Semantic reindex of 634 items in 41s. Keyword, semantic (cosine 0.55-0.59), hybrid (0.89 top hit) all working. Added exponential backoff to embedding batch retries.
Phase 5 (Calendar): All 4 views (agenda/day/week/month) x 3 formats verified. Recurrence expansion works (weekly meeting events). Edge cases (empty/past/future) handled correctly.
Phase 6 (SDK): 2 extensions active (beads, todos). Extension doctor passes. --no-extensions isolation works. Contracts command verified.
Phase 7 (Output): TOON/JSON/quiet/profile modes all verified. Error guidance follows What/Why/Required/Examples/NextSteps pattern consistently.
Phase 8 (Telemetry): Stack healthy (9/9 containers up, 5411 events/24h, 32464 total events, 1356 installs). Sentry has 5 active issues correlated with telemetry. Analysis report created.
Phase 9 (UX): Enhanced placeholder ID detection in error guidance. Top 3 telemetry errors (214 events) have clear, actionable guidance. pm-sh4x closed.
Phase 10 (Governance): All findings tracked as pm items. Completed items closed with evidence.

notes.author: audit-agent
notes.text: Audit methodology: (1) Security scan via check-secrets.mjs + grep for credentials (2) Sandbox dogfood in /tmp with full lifecycle exercise (3) Code quality analysis via grep for duplicated patterns (4) SDK review of exports + extension examples (5) Search verification with semantic queries against real items (6) Calendar test of all 4 views x 3 formats (7) Telemetry Postgres analysis from remote stack (8) Performance profiling via time command + telemetry P50/P95 data

learnings.author: audit-agent
learnings.text: Telemetry data from real users is the most valuable signal for prioritization. The top 3 error messages (no update flags, invalid status closed, undefined ID) each suggest specific UX improvements that would prevent confusion. Performance P95 data reveals search and health as the two highest-impact optimization targets.

comments.author: maintainer-agent
comments.text:
Dogfood audit (2026-05-01) completed. Findings summary:

FIXED IN THIS SESSION:
- pm-on3q: Telemetry queue oversized-event pruning gap (60MB queue drained to ~0 after fix to pruneExpiredQueueEntries)
- pm-1h7w: Priority --priority error message lacked 0..4 range and semantic labels
- pm-8r2r: pm files --add bare path gave misleading error implying scope is required
- pm-itb0: Calendar --include scheduled alias missing (summary uses 'scheduled', filter required 'events')
- pm-dc2y: pm templates bare command showed empty output instead of template list

WORKING WELL (verified in dogfood):
- All list variants (list, list-all, list-open, list-draft, list-in-progress, list-blocked, list-closed, list-canceled)
- Calendar: deadline, reminder, event, recurring event support all working correctly
- SDK ExtensionApi: comprehensive (14+ register methods, 5 hooks, search providers, vector stores)
- pm context: excellent agent-optimized snapshot
- pm contracts: 56 commands machine-readable
- pm search: hybrid mode working
- Extension scaffold: CommonJS by design, works correctly
- Telemetry endpoint: receiving events at pm-cli.unbrained.dev/v1/events, UUID validation, ok responses

OPEN TRACKING ITEMS:
- pm-ne67: Activity --compact mode for agents
- pm-gmnh: Health ok:false for normal telemetry queue draining
- pm-vg5h: Recurring event unbounded expansion in calendar
- pm-b1pd: Calendar recurring event double-title output redundancy
- pm-g8gj: Verify remote telemetry stack Grafana dashboards

Test suite: 1087 tests at 100% coverage maintained throughout.

notes.author: codex-audit

notes.author: codex-agent
notes.text: Pi package research snapshot 2026-05-11: pi.dev/packages advertises npm-published package catalog entries and installs with pi install npm:<package>. Current Pi docs define packages as bundles of extensions, skills, prompt templates, and themes; package.json pi manifest supports extensions/skills/prompts/themes arrays plus gallery image/video; conventional directories are auto-discovered if no pi manifest exists. Sources include npm, git URLs/shorthands, and local paths; project-local installs use -l and persist to .pi/settings.json; temporary try-run uses -e. Pi package manager dedupes project over global, supports filtering per resource type, pins versioned npm/git refs, runs npm install for npm/git packages, and loads resources through a central resource-loader.

notes.author: codex-agent
notes.text: External research refresh 2026-05-11: Pi packages bundle extensions, skills, prompts, and themes; support npm/git/local sources; run npm install for npm/git packages; use package.json pi manifests or convention directories; support per-resource filters, project-over-global dedupe, enable/disable controls, and explicit security warnings. OpenClaw separates small core from npm/community plugins, supports local/archive/npm/marketplace installs, list/inspect/doctor/enable/disable, discovery precedence, plugin slots, manifest-declared capabilities, and focused SDK subpath imports. Implication for pm: keep item/storage/history/config/output/package loader primitives in core; move optional workflows/resources/integrations behind installable packages with manifest contracts and diagnostics.

notes.author: codex-agent
notes.text: Reference architecture distilled for pm: small core keeps project-management primitives, storage, history, config, contracts, output, telemetry, and package loader; optional custom workflows ship as npm/git/local packages with manifests, managed state, diagnostics, enable/disable, and upgrade paths. Pi/OpenClaw are references only; no agent-harness semantics belong in pm core.

notes.author: codex-agent
notes.text: Detailed package extraction plan from 2026-05-14 codebase audit: 1) pm-calendar: move calendar/cal views, reminders/events/recurrence expansion, and schedule presets behind package commands while keeping item deadline fields core. 2) pm-search-advanced: keep keyword search core, move semantic/hybrid reindex, embedding providers, vector stores, cache refresh, and advanced search provider defaults to installable search packages. 3) pm-linked-test-runner: keep linked test metadata core, move subprocess execution, background runs, assertion policies, PM-context sandboxing, and test-runs lifecycle to a runner package. 4) pm-governance-audit: move dedupe-audit, comments-audit, normalize sweeps, advanced validate checks, and broad update-many governance workflows to a governance package. 5) pm-guide-shell: keep help core, move guide topics, completion generation, and shell-specific UX to a docs/shell package. 6) pm-templates: move templates save/list/show and create --template acceleration into a templates package. Cross-cutting requirement: every extraction must preserve SDK primitives, dynamic contracts/MCP availability, package TypeScript sources, temp-project dogfood, and release/observability gates.

notes.author: sdk-calendar-search-subagent
notes.text: SDK/package/calendar/search architecture review evidence 2026-05-25: package-first boundary is mostly preserved in current dirty checkout. Positive checks: package.json now exports ./sdk/runtime; bundled package module capabilities are aligned by package-manifest regression; calendar package validates invalid --format/conflicting --json+--format before running calendar core and returns usage exit 2 for repeated positional views; search-advanced JS manifest now includes schema capability; LanceDB local snapshot cache now stats snapshot mtime/size and reloads when another process deletes or rewrites backing JSON. Focused tests passed: pnpm test -- --run tests/unit/calendar-command.spec.ts tests/unit/package-manifest.spec.ts tests/unit/schema-command.spec.ts tests/unit/sdk-index.spec.ts tests/unit/vector-store-adapter.spec.ts (73 tests); pnpm test -- --run tests/unit/completion-command.spec.ts tests/unit/contracts-command.spec.ts (109 tests). Isolated runtime smoke with PM_PATH/PM_GLOBAL_PATH temp + pm init --defaults --with-packages verified runtime contracts for reindex/completion and calendar markdown output. Finding to carry in package-first work: shell completion generation is still partly static/hardcoded and not package/runtime-contract driven end to end. Bash top-level command list comes from PM_CORE_COMMAND_NAMES and omits installed package commands, while zsh/fish descriptions still unconditionally advertise package commands such as calendar/reindex/guide/completion. Bash accepts runtime field flags for only list/create/update/update-many/search/calendar/context; zsh/fish do not consume runtime command_flags. Recommendation: make completion derive command catalog, package command availability, aliases, and flags from runtime contracts/active registrations, then render all shells from the same contract model.
