comments.author: codex-store-index-specialist-2026-05-23
comments.text:
Store/index cost specialist evidence 2026-05-23:
Scope: read-only source investigation plus timing probes; no source edits. Bootstrap verified with npm install -g ., pm --version => 2026.5.23, node -v => v25.9.0, pnpm -v => 10.33.0, pnpm build => pass.
Commands/probes: pm context/search/list-in-progress confirmed reuse of pm-gt82; inspected src/core/store/item-store.ts, src/core/store/front-matter-cache.ts, src/cli/commands/list.ts, search.ts, context.ts, get.ts, tests/unit/front-matter-cache.spec.ts, item-store.spec.ts, list-command.spec.ts, context-command.spec.ts. Runtime artifact check: .agents/pm/runtime/metadata-cache.json is ~4.5MB with 829 cached entries and cached bodies; search/vectorization-status.json and local lancedb exist, but .agents/pm/index/manifest.json is absent.
Timing on warmed checkout with telemetry/sentry disabled: shell pm list-open --limit 5 --json ~0.34-0.54s; pm context --limit 5 --json ~0.44-0.70s; pm search startup --limit 5 --json ~0.56s; pm get pm-gt82 --fields id,title,status,updated_at --json ~0.41s first shell probe, ~0.22s later. In-process dist probes isolate store cost: listAllFrontMatter warm ~142-156ms for 829 entries; runList open limit5 ~224ms; runSearch startup limit5 ~172ms; runContext brief limit5 ~237ms; runGet existing fields ~7-17ms, default get ~39ms; missing get did-you-mean path ~141ms.
Findings: list/search/context are scan-bound because front-matter-cache still readdir's all type folders, stats every item file, runs onRead hooks for every file, reconstructs/sorts all candidates, and persists a full cache envelope on every read before command-level filters/limits apply. list applies limit only after full load/filter/sort. context calls runList once and for standard/deep can call runList a second time, compounding the same scan. keyword search also loads all cached documents and scores all filtered documents; the cache avoids parsing unchanged files but is not an index for status/type/tag/parent/updated_at/title/body tokens. get for an existing id is not item-scan-bound in core; it uses locateItem path probes plus one item read and optional history read. get missing-id suggestions still scan known ids.
Recommended low-risk implementation: add a store-owned runtime index layer in src/core/store/front-matter-cache.ts or a new src/core/store/item-index.ts that persists sorted rows keyed by id and path, includes secondary maps for status/type/tags/parent/assignee/deadline/updated_at plus a lightweight token index for title/description/body/comments/notes/learnings, and exposes query helpers used by list/search/context before falling back to current listAll* functions. Keep the existing cache parser as the rebuild source; add a stale check using directory entry count plus per-path mtime/ctime/size, and only rewrite the cache/index when entries changed. Preserve runActiveOnReadHooks semantics by firing hooks only for returned/read rows by default, with a compatibility/full-scan fallback for extension modes that require per-file reads. For get, optionally add an id->LocatedItem lookup from the same index and keep locateItem as fallback; optimize not-found suggestions from the id set.
Files/functions to edit next: src/core/store/front-matter-cache.ts:listAllDocumentCandidatesCached; src/core/store/item-store.ts:locateItem/listAllFrontMatter/listAllFrontMatterWithBody/listKnownItemIds; src/cli/commands/list.ts:runList; src/cli/commands/search.ts:loadDocuments/runSearch keyword path; src/cli/commands/context.ts:runContext; src/cli/commands/get.ts:runGet not-found path. Tests to extend: tests/unit/front-matter-cache.spec.ts, item-store.spec.ts, list-command.spec.ts, search-command.spec.ts, context-command.spec.ts, get-append-command.spec.ts. Main risks: extension onRead hook compatibility, stale cache/index after out-of-band file mutation, custom item type folder/format preference handling, and memory/cache growth from indexing full bodies.

comments.author: codex-startup-profiler-2026-05-23
comments.text: Startup profiling evidence 2026-05-23 by codex-startup-profiler-2026-05-23. Bootstrap: npm install -g . (already up to date), pm --version=2026.5.23, node -v=v25.9.0, pnpm -v=10.33.0, pnpm build passed. Duplicate check/orient: pm context --limit 10, pm search "startup profiling performance pm-gt82" --limit 10, pm list-open --limit 20, pm list-in-progress --limit 20; reused active pm-gt82, no new item created. Built-in --profile commands inspected: pm --profile --version and pm --profile --no-extensions --version print only 2026.5.23 because the fast path bypasses main/profile; pm --profile --help and --no-extensions --help print help only; pm --profile list --json --limit 1 => profile:extensions loaded=1 command_handlers=1, profile:command=list took_ms=351; pm --profile --no-extensions list --json --limit 1 => took_ms=334; pm --profile get pm-gt82 --depth brief => extensions loaded=1, get took_ms=174; no-extensions get => 173; pm --profile context --limit 3 => extensions loaded=1, context took_ms=490; no-extensions context => 485; pm --profile contracts --command list --flags-only --json => extensions loaded=1, contracts took_ms=170; no-extensions contracts => 214. Wall-clock hyperfine command: hyperfine --warmup 1 --runs 5 --export-json /tmp/pm-startup-hyperfine-20260523.json [12 requested command variants]. Medians: --version 43.5ms; --no-extensions --version 102.1ms (slower because --no-extensions misses the single-arg fast path); --help 146.5ms; --no-extensions --help 108.2ms; list --json --limit 1 385.9ms; no-extensions list 334.6ms; get pm-gt82 --depth brief 249.0ms; no-extensions get 243.3ms; context --limit 3 599.7ms; no-extensions context 536.9ms; contracts --command list --flags-only --json 251.5ms; no-extensions contracts 230.1ms. CPU/import-load spot check command: node --cpu-prof --cpu-prof-dir=/tmp/pm-startup-cpu-20260523 --cpu-prof-name={list,context,contracts}.cpuprofile dist/cli.js ... . Hot spots: list total_samples=270 with front-matter-cache.js 40 samples/14.8% (loadCache 5.9%, persistCache 5.6%), package_json_reader 5.9%, settings.js 4.4%, cli-contracts.js/main.js each 3.0%; context total_samples=359 with front-matter-cache.js 68/18.9% (loadCache 8.1%, persistCache 8.1%), buffer/path/fs-promises/string_decoder load/write cost, settings.js 3.6%; contracts total_samples=186 with package_json_reader 9.1%, settings.js 8.1%, esm loader/utils/cjs loader ~18.8% combined, cli-contracts.js 3.8%, main.js 2.7%. Findings: extension loading in this checkout is only one handler and usually costs about 5-60ms; it is not the main remaining blocker. Remaining startup cost is dominated by importing full cli/main for non-version commands, settings/package-json/ESM loader work, and list/context repeatedly loading and persisting metadata-cache even on read-only cache-hit paths.

(sorted[mid-1]+sorted[mid])/2 : sorted[mid]; return {min:sorted[0],median,avg:Number(avg.toFixed(1)),max:sorted[sorted.length-1]};}; const out={}; for (const [name,args] of cases){const normal=[]; const noext=[]; for(let i=0;i<20;i++){normal.push(run(args)); noext.push(run(['--no-extensions',...args]));} out[name]={normal:stats(normal),no_extensions:stats(noext)};} console.log(JSON.stringify(out,null,2));"

comments.author: maintainer-agent
comments.text: Evidence 2026-05-27: CPU profiles of 'pm get' (249ms) and 'pm list' (345ms) show the per-command cost is ~entirely Node ESM module resolution/compilation (compileSourceTextModule, getPackageScopeConfig, realpathSync, defaultResolve, deserializePackageJSON ~85-90ms) over ~170 unbundled tsc files — there is NO item-specific hot-path cost left in get. pm-gt82 mis-benchmarked the bundle on 'pm --version', which short-circuits in cli.ts BEFORE main.js loads, so the bundle looked slower; real commands (list/get/search/--help) would benefit. Correct approach: esbuild --bundle --splitting --format=esm --platform=node --packages=external (keep node_modules + dynamic extension import() external; --splitting preserves the 4 lazy register-* modules and per-command laziness). esbuild is NOT currently a devDependency. Compile cache (enableCompileCache) is already effective (~1.24x) and does NOT remove resolution cost. This is the single biggest remaining startup lever (~90ms on every command, ~185ms->~110ms startup). Deferred to its own focused PR with extension/MCP/version/sentry-sourcemap verification — too risky to bundle inside the perf+reliability PR.

comments.author: codex-subagent-dogfood-2026-05-23
comments.text:
Manual dogfood design sub-agent proposal (no code edits): after current changes, run this isolated temp-directory smoke to cover agent lifecycle + compactness without touching repo tracker data.

Commands:
cd <repo>
pnpm build
WORK=$(mktemp -d)
mkdir -p "$WORK/project"
printf "# manual dogfood\n" > "$WORK/project/README.md"
export PM_AUTHOR=codex-manual-dogfood-smoke-2026-05-23
export PM_PATH="$WORK/project/.agents/pm"
export PM_GLOBAL_PATH="$WORK/global"
PM="node <repo>/dist/cli.js"
$PM --json init --defaults --author "$PM_AUTHOR" --with-packages
$PM --json config project set test-result-tracking --policy enabled
$PM --json create --title "Dogfood dependency blocker" --description "Blocker used to verify dependency graph output." --type Task --status open --priority 2 --create-mode progressive
export BLOCKER_ID=<item.id from previous JSON>
$PM --json create --title "Dogfood compact lifecycle" --description "Manual smoke for create/update/test/files/docs/deps/search/plan/history compactness." --type Task --status open --priority 1 --tags dogfood,agent-ux,compact --acceptance-criteria "Lifecycle, linked evidence, deps, search, plan, and history compactness pass." --create-mode progressive --comment "created by isolated manual smoke"
export ROOT_ID=<item.id from previous JSON>
$PM --json update "$ROOT_ID" --status in_progress --estimate 20 --blocked-by "$BLOCKER_ID" --comment "start manual smoke and attach blocker"
$PM --json files "$ROOT_ID" --add "path=README.md,scope=project,note=temp project marker"
$PM --json docs "$ROOT_ID" --add "path=README.md,scope=project,note=temp docs marker"
$PM --json test "$ROOT_ID" --add "command=node -e \"console.log(\\\"linked smoke ok\\\")\",scope=project,timeout_seconds=30"
$PM --json test "$ROOT_ID" --run --progress --fail-on-skipped --fail-on-empty-test-run
$PM --json deps "$ROOT_ID" --format graph --summary
$PM --json deps "$ROOT_ID" --format tree --max-depth 2 --collapse repeated
$PM --json get "$ROOT_ID" --depth brief
$PM --json search "Dogfood compact lifecycle" --limit 5 --fields id,title,status,type
$PM --json search "Dogfood compact lifecycle" --include-linked --limit 5 --full
$PM --json history "$ROOT_ID" --limit 8
$PM --json history "$ROOT_ID" --full --limit 8
$PM --json history "$ROOT_ID" --verify
$PM --json plan create --title "Dogfood plan compactness" --scope "Manual Plan lifecycle smoke" --harness codex-manual --parent "$ROOT_ID"
export PLAN_ID=<plan.id from previous JSON>
$PM --json plan add-step "$PLAN_ID" --step-title "Exercise linked lifecycle" --step-body "Use linked files/docs/tests and deps"
$PM --json plan add-step "$PLAN_ID" --step-title "Verify compact surfaces" --depends-on "$ROOT_ID"
$PM --json plan update-step "$PLAN_ID" plan-step-001 --step-status in_progress --step-evidence "lifecycle commands started"
$PM --json plan complete-step "$PLAN_ID" plan-step-001 --step-evidence "linked lifecycle commands passed"
$PM --json plan decision "$PLAN_ID" --decision-text "Use compact defaults plus explicit full escape hatches" --decision-rationale "Agents need short loops and inspectable detail on demand"
$PM --json plan validation "$PLAN_ID" --validation-text "history verification remains ok" --validation-command "pm history <id> --verify"
$PM --json plan approve "$PLAN_ID"
$PM --json plan materialize "$PLAN_ID" --steps plan-step-002 --materialize-type Task --materialize-parent "$ROOT_ID"
$PM --json plan show "$PLAN_ID" --fields id,title,steps_summary
$PM --json history "$PLAN_ID" --verify
$PM --json close "$PLAN_ID" "plan manual smoke complete" --validate-close warn
$PM --json close "$BLOCKER_ID" "blocker graph smoke complete" --validate-close warn
$PM --json close "$ROOT_ID" "manual compact lifecycle smoke complete" --validate-close warn
$PM --json validate --check-resolution --check-history-drift
$PM --json health --check-only --no-refresh --brief

Why this covers agent workflows: it uses sandboxed PM_PATH/PM_GLOBAL_PATH, exercises create/update plus linked files/docs/tests, proves blocked-by deps are visible as summary and tree, compares compact/default read paths against --full escapes for search/history, runs the Plan create/step/decision/validation/materialize loop, verifies history chains, and finishes with validate/health gates. Keep the full scripts/dogfood-package-first.mjs harness for package/SDK/calendar coverage; this sequence is the short manual smoke for lifecycle and compactness regressions.

Decision/risk: no tiny fix required in this pass. Current observable risk is not a fresh secret leak; it is the standing pm-xk39 class of history/privacy hygiene, which remains the right lineage for future redaction work if a real leaked value is found.

learnings.author: codex-agent
learnings.text: Durable learning: strict 100% branch coverage is valuable for agent UX changes because nullable fallback expressions can silently introduce uncovered impossible branches; prefer explicit state predicates over defensive null coalescing in logically constrained paths.

learnings.author: codex-agent
learnings.text: Durable learning: when adding default terse projections for agent UX, preserve semantic override paths (include-body, explicit full/compact/fields) so feature-level defaults do not silently drop required payloads in existing workflows.

learnings.author: maintainer-agent
learnings.text: Startup perf: the single biggest CLI startup cost was importing zod (~85ms) eagerly via settings.ts, which is on the hot read path of nearly every command (list/get/search/context/create/update all readSettings). zod was used in exactly ONE file. Replacing it with a dependency-free validator (src/core/store/settings-validator.ts) that mirrors safeParse exactly cut settings import 157ms->14ms and every command ~38% (--help 227->140ms, list 540->340ms). Lesson: profile per-module import cost (node --input-type=module timing each import) before assuming the bottleneck is your own code; a single eager dep on the hot path dominates.

comments.text: Execution scope 2026-04-27 (mixed-wave): implement first-class normalize command in two slices. Slice A (dry-run): detect low-signal metadata/closure-like drift patterns and emit deterministic mutation previews sorted by item id/field, reusing governance policy primitives from validate and update-many planning semantics. Slice B (apply): execute approved normalization updates via safe mutation flow with explicit apply flag, preserving ownership semantics/history writes and avoiding non-deterministic side effects. Acceptance mapping: (1) scan/report = normalized findings + per-item planned field changes and summary counts; (2) deterministic preview = stable dry-run output contract suitable for agent automation (--json); (3) apply mode = history-backed updates through existing PM mutation pathways with clear result reporting and warnings.

notes.author: codex-agent
notes.text: Brainstorming source: user requested a pm plan feature comparable to Codex/Claude/Cursor plan surfaces,with easy dependency and related-item linking for agents.

notes.author: codex-agent
notes.text:
Concrete command sketch for `pm plan`:
- `pm plan create --title "..." --scope pm-xxxx --related pm-yyyy --claim --json` creates a durable plan attached to an item or request.
- `pm plan add-step <PLAN> --text "..." --depends-on pm-xxxx --related pm-yyyy --status pending` records a linked step.
- `pm plan update-step <PLAN> 2 --status in_progress --evidence "..."` should enforce a single active step unless `--allow-multiple-active` is passed.
- `pm plan complete-step <PLAN> 2 --evidence "tests passed"` is the harness-equivalent checklist completion mutation.
- `pm plan show <PLAN> --depth brief|standard|deep --fields id,current_step,linked_items --json` gives progressive-disclosure reads.
- `pm plan materialize <PLAN> --steps 2,3 --type Task --parent pm-xxxx` converts selected steps into real pm items and links them back with discovered_from/implements relationships.

Suggested JSON shape: `{ id, scope_item_id, title, status, current_step_id, steps:[{id, order, text, status, linked_items:[{id, kind}], evidence, updated_at}], summary:{pending,active,blocked,completed}, next_actions:[...] }`.

learnings.author: codex-agent
learnings.text: Existing `pm-planner` plugin assets are workflow guidance, not a durable first-class plan artifact. The durable gap is a core/SDK/MCP plan state model that agents can update like harness checklists while linking each step to pm dependencies and related work. Keep the MVP harness-neutral and expose stable JSON so Codex, Claude Code, Cursor, and plugins can adapt it without hardcoding one harness model into core.

comments.author: maintainer-agent
comments.text: Related UX inconsistency observed in same session: 'pm update pm-XXX --add-tags fix' rejects with 'missing: --tags'. The retry suggestion is even worse — it says 'pm update pm-ydkl --tags fix' which REPLACES all tags (losing existing ones) instead of adding. Either (a) pm update should accept --add-tags as it does in other places, or (b) the retry hint should preserve existing tags. Same root cause as the --expected vs --expected-result inconsistency: flag aliases that exist on one mutation but not another.

comments.author: maintainer-agent
comments.text: Branch feat/agent-ux-tag-mutations-and-result-aliases implements both halves of this issue: (1) --expected/--actual short aliases now accepted on pm update + pm create + pm update-many (matching pm close), wired through commander-mutation-options contracts + register-mutation + CLOSE/CREATE/UPDATE/UPDATE_MANY flag contracts in cli-contracts.ts. (2) Additive tag mutations: --add-tags (create/update/update-many) and --remove-tags (update/update-many) via new core helpers mergeAdditiveTags/applyTagRemovals/collectTagFlagValues in core/item/parse.ts (CSV + JSON-array entries, sorted+deduped+lowercased). NEW BUG FOUND & FIXED during dogfooding: update-many --add-tags/--remove-tags reported updated_count:0 skipped_count:N because buildPlannedItemDiff ignored additive tag ops -> empty diff -> every row skipped. Added buildTagMutationPlan() replicating runUpdate's tags resolution order (replace->add->remove) so dry-run preview AND apply-mode actionable detection both see additive tag changes. Removed now-dead tags scalar branch + splitCommaList import. Verified in temp dir: create/update/update-many/close all correct. 1970 unit tests green.

comments.author: maintainer-agent
comments.text: Sub-agent work (orchestrator-recorded to avoid concurrent history writes): (A) docs specialist updated docs/COMMANDS.md (+17: --tags REPLACES vs --add-tags/--remove-tags additive note + examples + --expected/--actual alias para), docs/AGENT_GUIDE.md (+7: canonical-loop accumulation note), docs/QUICKSTART.md (+3). Noted gap: close inline result fields (--resolution/--expected-result/--actual-result) still undocumented in these files (pre-existing; candidate follow-up). (B) completion specialist updated src/cli/commands/completion.ts zsh+fish for create/update/update-many (--add-tags, --remove-tags, --expected, --actual). Orchestrator additionally completed the close completion section which was missing ALL inline closure flags (--reason/--close-reason/--resolution/--expected-result/--actual-result + new --expected/--actual) in both zsh and fish — now matches CLOSE_FLAG_CONTRACTS. tsc clean.

comments.author: maintainer-agent
comments.text: PR #82 review round 1 addressed (commit 42e51d6e). Codex P2 #1: dropped list:true from add-tags/remove-tags contracts (bootstrap coalescer was corrupting JSON-array form of repeated occurrences). Codex P2 #2: added addTags/removeTags to tool schema (TOOL_*_OPTION_CONTRACTS + PM_TOOL_PARAMETER_PROPERTIES) for additionalProperties:false MCP/SDK clients. Gemini: lowercased baseTags in mergeAdditiveTags/applyTagRemovals (case-insensitive dedup+removal); pm update skips no-op tag writes. Declined gemini's normalizeExistingTags-lowercase in update-many (would skip legitimate case-normalization writes — RAW comparison is correct). Companion pm-changelog PR #20 round 1 also addressed (commit ee22d5d): strong(type/tag) vs weak(title) Changed-signal split so explicit refactor tag wins over Issue->Fixed default; typeof guard on item.type. Both: 100% coverage/all gates green. Re-triggered codex+gemini.

comments.author: maintainer-agent
comments.text: PR #82 review round 2 addressed (commit 56b6b49c). Codex P2: reverted the tag no-op write skip — pm has no no-op suppression anywhere (re-setting any scalar churns history by append-only audit design), so a tags-only exception was inconsistent + incomplete (mutateItem still wrote). Tags now always record a change like every other field. Codex P3: removed literal NUL bytes an earlier delimiter edit embedded (verified grep -rlP found none). Gemini medium x2: Array.isArray guard in collectTagFlagValues + non-string filter in normalizeBaseTags. Codex P3 CHANGELOG wording: entry is the item title (generated); classifier reclassification handled by pm-changelog PR #20. Round-1 case-insensitive + JSON-array fixes unchanged/re-verified. 1973 tests, 100% coverage, all gates green. Re-triggered codex+gemini.

comments.author: maintainer-agent
comments.text: PR #82 review round 3 addressed (commit c4dc3ed3). Codex P1: added --add-tags/--remove-tags to enforceAllowAuditUpdateScope dep-audit disallowed list (non-owner could mutate tags via --allow-audit-dep-update). Codex P2 x2: --unset tags now conflicts with --add-tags/--remove-tags on update+create; --add-tags/--remove-tags now counted in command_option_policies tags checks (disabled+required) in create (hasOptionValue) + update (mark). Declined gemini no-op-skip (system has no no-op suppression; reverted in round 2). New tests for all 3 guards. 100% coverage. Re-triggered codex+gemini.

comments.author: maintainer-agent
comments.text: DONE — PR #82 merged to main (merge commit 1174a12d) after 5 review rounds. Codex converged (no findings on final commits); CodeRabbit green; Sourcery green/rate-limited; Gemini's final 3 mediums consciously declined with reasoning (empty-string check mirrors --tags; standalone snake-alias style pre-exists in CREATE/UPDATE). Companion pm-changelog PR #20 merged (commit on main) — classifier strong/weak signal split so this item reclassifies Changed->Fixed on next regen. All CI green 12/12, 100% coverage. Shipped: --add-tags/--remove-tags (create/update/update-many), --expected/--actual aliases, update-many additive-tag skip bug fix, dep-audit + unset + command_option_policy guards for the new flags, MCP tool schema, docs, zsh/fish completions.

comments.author: maintainer-agent
comments.text: Root cause was NOT the telemetry probe (checkTelemetry defaults false). Two real causes: (a) ok:false because health computed ok = (warnings.length===0), so the advisory telemetry_queue_pending warning flipped overall health to not-ok; (b) the ~6.8s wall time was the history_drift check re-reading + JSON-parsing + hash-chain-verifying all 858 streams (17MB) every run. Fixes: (1) isAdvisoryHealthWarning() — telemetry_* warnings are surfaced but never flip ok (telemetry is opt-out, non-critical); (2) drift-scan.ts now caches per-stream verification keyed by history file mtime/size (runtime/history-drift-cache.json, atomic write) — unchanged streams skip re-read/re-verify, item-side changes still caught by recomputing the document hash. Measured: pm health 8453ms -> ~1300ms (6.5x), ok:true. New drift-scan.spec.ts gives drift-scan.ts 100% coverage (69/69 stmts, 36/36 branches); two health-command tests updated to assert advisory (ok stays true).

comments.author: claude-code-agent
comments.text: Implementation + drift cleanup complete (2026-05-22). (1) Shipped audited `pm history-repair <id>`: replays a stream from empty, recomputes every before/after hash, recovers legacy patch ops that no longer strictly apply (replace->add on first-write paths; skips unresolvable array-shape ops), reconciles the latest hash with the on-disk item, and appends an op=history_repair audit marker. Never modifies item content; safe no-op on clean streams; --dry-run/--author/--message/--force with the same ownership+lock governance as history-redact. (2) Consolidated replay/patch mechanics into src/core/history/replay.ts; history/restore/history-redact now share it (pm-pjs5) with per-command error wrappers preserved. (3) Edge case discovered+fixed: a legacy stream whose first entry is a partial update (pm-lty) replays to a non-canonicalizable doc; replayHash now falls back to a deterministic structural hash for such states (full docs unaffected). (4) Ran on all 31 drifted real streams: pm health now ok=true (was ok=false for months), validate --check-history-drift drifted=0. git diff shows only history JSONL changed; no item content touched. (5) check-secrets initially failed because the privacy sub-agent had quoted literal secret-pattern example strings into its pm-xk39 evidence comment; scrubbed via audited history-redact -> check-secrets passes.

Remaining recovery friction found in fresh temp project: `pm test-all --json --type Task` fails with unknown_option and no did-you-mean/alternative, although many list/search commands accept --type; `pm guide search --json` correctly says available topics but suggested_retry becomes `pm guide search --json --list "<value>"`, which appears malformed versus the message saying `pm guide --list`. comments.author: manual-dogfood-specialist
comments.text: Additional manual dogfood recovery finding 2026-05-25: `pm list-all --sort updated --order desc` fails with allowed values `priority|deadline|updated_at|created_at|title|parent`, but the JSON error examples include `pm list-all --json --fields priority --sort updated --order desc`, repeating the invalid value. This is another recovery/example correctness issue for agents; use `updated_at` in docs/examples or accept `updated` as an alias.

comments.author: claude-code-agent
comments.text: IMPLEMENTED (same branch). Four recovery improvements closing the dogfood findings: (1) Cross-command flag awareness: commander-usage.ts builds a flag->commands index from PM_CORE_COMMAND_NAMES (declaration order, alias/shortcut commands like ctx/list-* excluded); unknown_option errors now add 'X is a valid option on: create, list, list-all' (threaded via new CommanderGuidanceContext.unknownOptionOtherCommands -> error-guidance unknown_option nextSteps). Fixes `pm test-all --type` and `pm create --fields`. (2) Prefix/abbreviation-aware suggestNearestLongFlags: --desc now suggests --description (rank-0 prefix match ahead of levenshtein typos). (3) list --sort aliases: updated->updated_at, created->created_at (+update/create/modified) in list.ts parseSortField; help text + invalid-value error updated; fixes `pm list-all --sort updated`. (4) Semantic map extended: update/create --add-tags/--add-tag/--tag -> --tags. Existing --body->--add (comments/notes/learnings) still works. Tests: list-command (alias accept+reject), error-guidance (cross-command+nearest render), help-runtime (test-all --type cross-command hint, create --desc abbreviation). Manual temp-dir smoke confirmed all four + JSON envelope next_steps carries the hint. Note: guide-shell suggested_retry malformedness (a comment finding) lives in the pm-guide-shell package, out of core scope.

comments.author: claude-code-agent
comments.text: Execution start 2026-05-25 (claude-code-agent orchestrator): reproduced data loss in sandbox — 'pm create issue X --tag alpha --tag beta --tag gamma' persists only tags=[gamma]. Root cause confirmed: normalizeBootstrapInvocation (src/cli/bootstrap-args.ts) rewrites each '--tag V' to canonical '--tags V' via levenshtein typo match, then Commander's scalar --tags option keeps only the LAST occurrence. Same keep-last class affects genuine repeats of comma-list flags (--tags a --tags b) and list-filter comma flags (pm list --tag a --tag b, --status, --type, --priority, --fields). Fix lane: mark comma-list flags in CliFlagContract and coalesce repeated canonical list-flag occurrences into one comma-joined token at normalization time. Branch fix/agent-ux-repeated-list-flags-accumulate.

comments.author: claude-code-flag-engineer
comments.text:
Implemented argv-level coalescing of repeated comma-separated list flags (fixes the keep-last data loss).

Step 1 (cli-contracts.ts): added optional list?: boolean to CliFlagContract and marked 9 flags list:true -- CREATE --tags, UPDATE --tags, UPDATE_MANY --tags, PLAN --tags + PLAN --fields, LIST_FILTER --status + --fields, GET --fields, SEARCH --fields. Deliberately did NOT mark list.ts scalar filters --tag/--type/--priority (verified list.ts parses --tag as scalar), nor any repeatable:true seed flag (--dep/--comment use Commander variadic collector).

Step 2 (bootstrap-args.ts): added listCanonicalFlags:Set to FlagLookup (populated in buildFlagLookup using the same canonical formula as canonicalFlag), added list_merge to the reason union, and a pure exported coalesceRepeatedListFlags(argv,listFlags) that walks argv L->R, merges repeats of a canonical list flag into the FIRST occurrence as one --flag=v1,v2,v3 token, drops later flag+value tokens, handles both '--flag value' and '--flag=value' forms, stops at --, and leaves value-less occurrences untouched. Wired it after the per-token loop in normalizeBootstrapInvocation; events pushed into trace.

DESIGN DECISION: coalesce at argv-normalization time rather than switching these flags to Commander variadic collectors. Rationale: (a) the root cause is normalizeBootstrapInvocation rewriting --tag->--tags per-occurrence, so the singular-typo case must be handled in this layer anyway; (b) downstream parsers (parseTags, list/get/search field+status) already split a single comma string, so a merged token flows through unchanged with zero downstream edits; (c) Commander variadic would change option types/contracts/MCP schemas broadly and not fix the singular --tag typo path; (d) single-occurrence inputs stay byte-identical (no = rewrite), preserving existing behavior.

VERIFICATION: pnpm build clean. Targeted tests: 52 passed (bootstrap-args.spec.ts + new list-flag-accumulation.integration.spec.ts). Manual proof in isolated sandbox: create issue X --tag a --tag b --tag c -> get item.tags=[a,b,c]; create issue Y --tags p --tags q -> get item.tags=[p,q].

comments.author: claude-code-flag-engineer
comments.text:
Full gate results (all green):

- node scripts/run-tests.mjs coverage: 121 test files, 1797 tests passed. Coverage 100% statements (2493/2493), 100% branches (1810/1810), 100% functions (463/463), 100% lines (2400/2400).
- pnpm quality:static: Static quality gate passed. (changed files within limits: cli-contracts.ts 1818 LOC, bootstrap-args.ts 678, bootstrap-args.spec.ts 381, list-flag-accumulation.integration.spec.ts 39; left cli.integration.spec.ts at 6989 untouched to avoid crossing the 7000 test-line gate, hence the new sibling integration spec.)
- node scripts/check-secrets.mjs: No credential-like secrets detected in tracked files.

Tests added: 13 unit cases for normalizeBootstrapInvocation (repeated singular typo, repeated plural, list-filter --status, repeated --fields, mixed space+inline, single occurrence unchanged, non-list --title not merged, -- terminator) and coalesceRepeatedListFlags (empty set passthrough, multi-flag independent merge, order preservation, value-less trailing flag, following-flag-not-a-value, -- remainder verbatim), plus a list_merge trace-event shape assertion ('--tags (x3)' -> ['--tags=a,b,c']). 2 integration regression cases run the real built dist.

No deviations from spec except: integration regression placed in a new sibling spec file tests/integration/list-flag-accumulation.integration.spec.ts (not appended to cli.integration.spec.ts) because that file is already 6989 lines and the static gate test limit is 7000 -- adding to it would risk breaking the gate. No new src files were added (coverage-include allowlist untouched). package.json version unchanged; item left in_progress for review.

comments.author: claude-code-agent
comments.text: Orchestrator finalization (claude-code-agent): (1) Added --group-by (aggregate) to the list:true set after verifying parseGroupBy splits commas and repeated --group-by previously kept-last; sandbox confirms 'aggregate --group-by status --group-by type' now matches the comma form. (2) Independent regression: dedicated dogfood sub-agent ran a 28/28 PASS full-workflow sweep on the rebuilt dist (init/create all types/list/get/claim/update/close/restore/comments/notes/learnings/files/docs/context/search/contracts/aggregate/stats/history --verify/validate/health/plan/deps/schema) with ZERO breakage; history --verify ok, validate ok, health ok. (3) Final gates green: node scripts/run-tests.mjs coverage = 121 files / 1797 tests / 100% statements,branches,functions,lines; pnpm quality:static passed; check-secrets clean. (4) Dogfood surfaced two NEW on-theme follow-ups (no duplicates, dedup evidence recorded): pm-ch59 (create/update changed_fields output token bloat — deferred: 66 changed_fields test refs + create-vs-update design call warrants its own PR) and pm-8nyc (unknown-option did-you-mean / cross-command flag confusion — enhancement). Branch fix/agent-ux-repeated-list-flags-accumulate.

comments.author: claude-code-agent
comments.text: PR #70 review follow-up (Codex P2): coalesceRepeatedListFlags greedily consumed the token after a list-flag as its value, which could misread a list-flag-looking token that is actually the value of a value-consuming option (notably global --path, which accepts values beginning with --). Repro before fix: 'pm --path --tags create issue X --tags a --tags b' swallowed the 'create' command token -> 'Unknown command issue'. Fix: coalesce now skips the value token of value-consuming flags (GLOBAL_VALUE_CONSUMING_FLAGS={--path}; other globals are boolean) so a --prefixed value is never reinterpreted as a flag nor swallows the following command/positional. Added regression unit test. Verified: '--path --tags create issue X --tags a --tags b' now parses create + tags=[a,b] (path='--tags' -> correct 'Tracker not initialized'); normal repeated --tag still accumulates. Gates green: 121 files/1798 tests/100% coverage, static quality, secrets clean. CodeRabbit/Sourcery rate-limited (no findings); Gemini: no issues.

comments.author: claude-code-agent
comments.text: FIXED (pm-uz25 step 1). Root cause: pm deps builds the graph only from the dependencies[] array, but update.ts --blocked-by wrote only the scalar blocked_by field (create.ts already added the edge; update.ts was asymmetric). Fix in src/cli/commands/update.ts: (1) new reconcileBlockedByDependency() helper; (2) resolve the --blocked-by target via locateItem before the synchronous mutate callback; (3) in the blocked_by mutate block, add a {kind:'blocked_by'} dependency edge when the target resolves, remove it on --unset blocked-by, and replace the stale edge on repoint. Verified by manual temp-dir smoke (edge_count 1->0) and 3 regression tests in update-command.spec.ts. update.ts is coverage-excluded so the 100% gate is unaffected.

comments.text: Implementation 2026-05-26 on branch dedup-history-rewrite-and-calendar-positional-recovery: replaced plain Error throw with a standalone class PmCliError (declared inside the package to keep it self-contained when installed outside the pm-cli source tree; the class name lines up with the Sentry beforeSend filter isExpectedCliErrorEvent so future PM-CLI-Z occurrences are filtered out). New error includes received args, optional 'Unknown view alias(es)' note, and two concrete recovery commands (single positional + --view flag). Verified all 4 affected test files pass (47/47): builtin-extension-entrypoints, calendar-command, sentry-helpers, cli-main-errors. Manual temp-dir dogfood confirmed: single positional view works, two positionals shows full recovery hint with exit 2.

comments.author: audit-agent
comments.text: Audit finding: bundled extensions (beads, todos) intentionally use ../../../../src/core/... internal imports since they ship as part of the repo and need direct access to internal APIs. Third-party/external extensions should use @unbrained/pm-cli/sdk public API. This is by-design for bundled extensions and not a bug. The SDK already exports defineExtension() and all necessary types for external extension development. Both extensions also contain local toNonEmptyString copies which follow the same pattern as internal CLI code.

SCOPE: git-tracked files only (2087 total; 1675 under .agents/), incl 700+ history JSONL streams and all .toon items. RESULTS: (1) node scripts/check-secrets.mjs => exit 0, 'No credential-like secrets detected'. (2) gitignore confirmed: 'git ls-files scripts/prod' and 'git ls-files .env' both EMPTY (untracked). (3) IPv4 scan: ZERO real/private IPs in tracked files; only IPv4-shaped token across entire .agents tree is 127.0.0.1 (6x, localhost). Historical 16-file private-IP leak (pm-2326/pm-ny6y) confirmed already remediated to public domain names. (4) /home//Users absolute paths: ZERO in .agents; the only /home/ hits are test fixtures in tests/*.spec.ts asserting redaction logic (excluded by check-secrets excludeFiles ^tests/). (5) API tokens/keys: ZERO matches for AKIA/ghp_/gho_/sk-/sntrys_/sntryu_/glpat-/xox_/[pattern]/Bearer/npm_ anywhere in tracked files or .agents. (6) password/secret assignments: ZERO real hits. (7) Hostnames: pm-cli.unbrained.dev (13x, public telemetry API - intentional config, also in src/core/shared/constants.ts:201) and pm.unbrained.dev (5x, public Grafana hostname referenced in audit evidence) - both public DNS, NOT secrets, NO credentials attached. /tmp/pm-*-audit paths are ephemeral, non-sensitive. CANDIDATES & CLASSIFICATION: Total candidates examined ~6 unique. REAL_SECRET=0. FALSE_POSITIVE=all: [a] src/core/sentry/instrument.ts:129 Sentry DSN https://bf7...@o4510...ingest.de.sentry.io/4511... = client-side public ingest key (ships in npm package; grants event-send only, no data read); NOT in check-secrets ruleset (which only flags sntryu_/sntrys_ API tokens) = INTENTIONAL public config. [b] *.unbrained.dev hostnames = public infra DNS = intentional config. [c] [pattern] in tests/*.spec.ts = test fixtures. [d] 127.0.0.1 = localhost. REDACTIONS PERFORMED: NONE (no confirmed real secret in any tracked file). VERDICT: CLEAN. No files modified.

comments.author: release-ci-subagent
comments.text:
release-ci-subagent DONE.

FILES CHANGED (2):
1) .github/workflows/auto-release.yml -- inserted a new step between 'Install dependencies' and 'Run auto release pipeline':
      - name: Build
        run: pnpm build
   This builds dist/cli.js so the release pipeline (run-release-pipeline.mjs lines ~284-302) can call 'dist/cli.js install npm:pm-changelog --project' and 'dist/cli.js changelog generate' without Cannot-find-module errors. Verified step order is now Install dependencies -> Build -> Run auto release pipeline; YAML parses cleanly.

2) tests/integration/release-automation-contract.spec.ts -- added regression test 'builds dist before the auto-release pipeline consumes dist/cli.js' (placed right after the existing auto-release workflow contract test). It reads auto-release.yml, asserts it contains 'pnpm build', and asserts indexOf('pnpm build') < indexOf('scripts/release/run-release-pipeline.mjs') so the build always precedes the pipeline. Matches existing indexOf style in the file.

TESTS RUN:
  node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/integration/release-automation-contract.spec.ts
  => 1 file passed, 11 tests passed (was 10, +1 new). Build runs first then tests; all green.

NOT TOUCHED (per constraints): CHANGELOG.md, src/cli/commands/config.ts (other agent), scripts/release/run-release-pipeline.mjs (workflow-only fix is sufficient; pipeline assumes dist/cli.js exists, which is the correct contract for a release job).

COORDINATOR TODO:
  - Add CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] line, e.g. under Fixed:
    'Fixed daily Auto Release workflow failing since 2026-05-24 by adding the missing pnpm build step before the release pipeline, so dist/cli.js exists when pm-changelog generation runs (pm-yf8t).'
  - Do NOT close pm-yf8t until PR merges (per instructions).

comments.author: claude-code-agent
comments.text: AI-bot review (all CI green: gates+tests+CodeRabbit+Sourcery+Gemini+codex). Actionable feedback to address: (1) gemini HIGH: type-guard document.metadata.blocked_by before .trim() in update.ts (crash risk; close.ts already guards). (2) coderabbit/codex MAJOR: semantic-degraded warning says results_are_lexical, but pure semantic mode returns empty hits (only hybrid includes keyword hits) -> make accurate / graceful. (3) coderabbit MAJOR: --status added to LIST_FILTER_FLAG_CONTRACTS but maybe not to TOOL_LIST_FILTER_OPTION_CONTRACTS -> verify list tool schema sync. (4) sourcery: duration path rejects equal start/end (zero duration) vs instant-event semantics -> align. (5) gemini/coderabbit: close.ts clear blocked_reason even without blocked_by + clear orphan blocked_by edges if scalar missing. (6) gemini HIGH: unresolved --blocked-by sets scalar+no edge (asymmetry) -> warn (never-block, mirrors parent-missing pattern). (7) sourcery/gemini: extract duplicated resolveEventEndAt to shared module. (8) coderabbit minor: CHANGELOG attribution pm-uzmf/CAL1. (9) coderabbit: harden S1 test with count/id. NOTE: coderabbit '.agents/pm direct edit' is a false positive (changes are pm-CLI-generated, not hand edits).

comments.author: audit-agent
comments.text:
AUDIT RESULTS SUMMARY (2026-04-26):

OVERALL STATUS: PASS - All 78 test files, 1052 tests pass. CLI fully functional.

DOGFOOD VERIFICATION (temp sandbox):
- init: OK - Creates all directories, schema files, settings
- config: OK - project/global scope, definition-of-done, test-result-tracking
- health: OK - All 8 checks pass (settings, directories, extensions, storage, integrity, history_drift, vectorization, settings_values)
- create: OK - All types (Epic, Feature, Task, Chore), all fields including rich metadata (risk, confidence, sprint, release, reviewer, goal, objective, value, impact, outcome, why_now)
- lifecycle: OK - claim, start-task, pause-task, close-task, release all work correctly
- update: OK - All fields update properly, history recorded
- comments/notes/learnings: OK - Append correctly with author/timestamp
- files/docs: OK - Link management works
- append: OK - Body append works
- calendar: OK - All 4 views (agenda, day, week, month), reminders and deadlines render correctly
- context: OK - Token-efficient snapshot with high/low level items and agenda
- aggregate: OK - Group-by type/status/priority works
- list variants: OK - list-all, list-open, list-in-progress, list-blocked, list-closed, list-canceled
- search: OK - Hybrid search with scoring and matched_fields
- reindex: OK - Keyword mode rebuilds artifacts
- history: OK - Full audit trail with JSON patches and hash chains
- activity: OK - Cross-item activity log
- deps: OK - Tree format dependency view
- stats: OK - Project statistics
- validate: OK - Resolution and history drift checks
- dedupe-audit: OK - Parent scope deduplication
- comments-audit: OK - Coverage reporting by type
- contracts: OK - Machine-readable flag/schema contracts
- search: OK - Hybrid mode with semantic weight

TELEMETRY STACK (remote): FULLY OPERATIONAL
- Endpoint: [redacted_telemetry_endpoint] returns HTTP 200 with proper payload
- [redacted_datastore]: [redacted_event_count] events stored and queryable
- [redacted_queue_service]: Healthy, message processing
- [redacted_monitoring_ui]: v11.6.14, dashboard accessible at [redacted_monitoring_endpoint]
- [redacted_log_store]: Ready, collecting container logs via [redacted_collector]
- [redacted_trace_store]: Ready, receiving OTLP traces
- [redacted_metrics_service]: Ready
- [redacted_collector]: v1.16.0, configured for Docker log discovery + OTLP trace forwarding

notes.author: audit-agent
notes.text:
FINDINGS - Issues and Improvements Identified:

1. TELEMETRY TEST ROBUSTNESS (FIXED): telemetry-runtime.spec.ts was vulnerable to env contamination - PM_TELEMETRY_DISABLED leaking from parent shell caused 4/5 tests to fail. Fixed by clearing env in withTempGlobalRoot helper.

2. SDK GAPS (existing items track these):
   - No extension project template/scaffold (only inline docs examples)
   - defineExtension is the only author helper utility
   - Custom field/search/vector adapter types are Record<string,unknown> not strongly typed
   - No deactivate/cleanup lifecycle hook for long-lived hosts
   - No public SDK for host lifecycle (load/activate) for third-party embedders

3. CALENDAR GAPS (minor, existing items track):
   - No local timezone display support (UTC-only bucketing)
   - No reminder recurrence (only events recur)
   - No iCal/CSV feed export
   - --limit is blunt global cap (can hide later days in month view)
   - Two different CalendarEvent types (storage vs output) share same name

4. TELEMETRY GAPS (some tracked):
   - capture_level setting stored but not enforced in runtime
   - retention_days setting stored but no local TTL cleanup
   - No background drainer (queue only flushes on next command)
   - No max queue size / disk cap
   - OTLP trace/span ID encoding may not match strict spec (hex vs base64)
   - No OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_HEADERS support for auth
   - pm health has no telemetry check (docs claim it does)
   - No CLI metrics for [redacted_metrics_service] (only traces via OTLP)
   - No crash/last-gasp flush

5. GENERAL:
   - All community files present (LICENSE, CHANGELOG, CONTRIBUTING, SECURITY, CODE_OF_CONDUCT)
   - .gitignore properly excludes telemetry data, search cache, .env
   - Default telemetry endpoint in constants.ts is appropriate (public endpoint)
   - No private IPs or credentials in source code

learnings.author: audit-agent
learnings.text:
Key learnings from 2026-04-26 dogfood audit:

1. The pm CLI is production-ready with 1052 passing tests across 78 files covering all features.
2. The extension system (SDK + runtime) is well-designed but needs scaffolding tools for extension authors.
3. Calendar is fully functional for agent use with 4 views, recurrence, reminders, and rich filtering.
4. Telemetry pipeline is end-to-end operational with [redacted_event_count] events in [redacted_datastore], but capture_level and retention_days settings are not enforced in code.
5. Test robustness requires explicit env cleanup - PM_TELEMETRY_DISABLED contamination caused false failures.
6. The TOON output format is excellent for agent token efficiency.
7. Remote stack ([redacted_observability_stack]) is fully operational.
8. All community files present and .gitignore properly configured for telemetry privacy.

comments.author: maintainer-agent
comments.text:
Implemented docs-first OpenAI response-order normalization increment for provider abstraction.

Docs updates:
- PRD.md now states provider response normalization includes deterministic OpenAI data-entry index ordering.
- README.md mirrors that contract in the planned-search/provider abstraction baseline.

Code + tests:
- src/core/search/providers.ts now normalizes OpenAI embedding entries with deterministic index ordering when index fields are present, and preserves original response position as deterministic tie-break for duplicate indexes.
- src/core/search/providers.ts validates explicit index fields using TypeError and extracts ordering logic into a helper for lint compliance.
- tests/unit/embedding-provider.spec.ts adds regressions for index-ordered normalization, duplicate-index tie-break behavior, and malformed mixed index payload rejection.

Verification evidence:
1) node dist/cli.js test pm-yv2 --run --timeout 3600 --json

   - node scripts/run-tests.mjs coverage passed with 100% lines/branches/functions/statements (including src/core/search/providers.ts at 100%).
